\"\"\"Prompt for step 'generate-{agent.id}'.\n   107→\n   108→    Instructs Claude to read the extraction document and generate a\n   109→    complete project roadmap with the agent's persona as a Unresolved references emit warnings but\n    42→    do not block the pipeline (warning-only mode per OQ-001 for v2.20).\n    43→    \"\"\"\n    44→    import re\n    45→    import warnings\n    46→\n    47→    # Extract heading anchors (simplified: heading text, lowercased)\n    48→    headings: set[str] = set()\n    49→    for line in content.splitlines():\n    50→        stripped = line.lstrip()\n    51→        if stripped.startswith(\"#\"):\n    52→            heading_text = stripped.lstrip(\"#\").strip().lower()\n    53→            headings.add(heading_text)\n    54→\n    55→    # Find cross-references like \"See section X\" or \"(see X.Y)\"\n    56→    refs = re.findall(r'[Ss]ee\\s+(?:[Ss]ection\\s+)?[\"\\']?(\\d+(?:\\.\\d+)*)', content)\n    57→    # If there are no cross-references, that's fine\n    58→    if not refs:\n    59→        return True\n    60→\n    61→    # Check each reference against headings\n    62→    unresolved: list[str] = []\n    63→    for ref in refs:\n    64→        found = any(ref in h for h in headings)\n    65→        if not found:\n    66→            # Also check if the section number appears as a heading prefix\n    67→            found = any(h.startswith(ref) for h in headings)\n    68→        if not found:\n    69→            unresolved.append(ref)\n    70→\n    71→    if unresolved:\n    72→        for ref in unresolved:\n    73→            warnings.warn(\n    74→                f\"Unresolved cross-reference: 'See section {ref}' has no matching heading\",\n    75→                stacklevel=2,\n    76→            )\n    77→        # Warning-only mode (OQ-001): return True to avoid blocking pipeline\n    78→        return True\n    79→\n    80→    return True\n    81→\n    82→\n    83→def _no_duplicate_headings(content: str) -> bool:\n    84→    \"\"\"No duplicate H2 or H3 heading text.\"\"\"\n    85→    seen: dict[int, set[str]] = {2: set(), 3: set()}\n    86→    for line in content.splitlines():\n    87→        stripped = line.lstrip()\n    88→        if stripped.startswith(\"## \") and not stripped.startswith(\"### \"):\n    89→            text = stripped[3:].strip().lower()\n    90→            if text in seen[2]:\n    91→                return False\n    92→            seen[2].add(text)\n    93→        elif stripped.startswith(\"### \"):\n    94→            text = stripped[4:].strip().lower()\n    95→            if text in seen[3]:\n    96→                return False\n    97→            seen[3].add(text)\n    98→    return True\n    99→\n   100→\n   101→def _frontmatter_values_non_empty(content: str) -> bool:\n   102→    \"\"\"All YAML frontmatter fields have non-empty values.\"\"\"\n   103→    stripped = content.lstrip()\n   104→    if not stripped.startswith(\"---\"):\n   105→        return False\n   106→\n   107→    rest = stripped[3:].lstrip(\"\\n\")\n   108→    end_idx = rest.find(\"\\n---\")\n   109→    if end_idx == -1:\n   110→        return False\n   111→\n   112→    frontmatter_text = rest[:end_idx]\n   113→    for line in frontmatter_text.splitlines():\n   114→        line = line.strip()\n   115→        if \":\" in line:\n   116→            _key, value = line.split(\":\", 1)\n   117→            if not value.strip():\n   118→                return False\n   119→    return True\n   120→\n   121→\n   122→def _has_actionable_content(content: str) -> bool:\n   123→    \"\"\"At least one section contains numbered or bulleted items.\"\"\"\n   124→    import re\n   125→    # 100 KB\n    51→\n    52→\n    53→def _embed_inputs(input_paths: list[Path]) -> str:\n    54→    \"\"\"Read input files and return their contents as fenced code blocks.\n    55→\n    56→    Each file is wrapped in a fenced block with a ``# <path>`` header.\n    57→    Returns an empty string when *input_paths* is empty (no-op).\n    58→    \"\"\"\n    59→    if not input_paths:\n    60→        return \"\"\n    61→\n    62→    blocks: list[str] = []\n    63→    for p in input_paths:\n    64→        content = Path(p).read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    65→        blocks.append(f\"# {p}\\n```\\n{content}\\n```\")\n    66→    return \"\\n\\n\".join(blocks)\n    67→\n    68→\n    69→def _sanitize_output(output_file: Path) -> int:\n    70→    \"\"\"Strip conversational preamble before YAML frontmatter in a step output file.\n    71→\n    72→    Reads the file, finds the first ``^---`` line, and removes everything before\n    73→    it.  Uses atomic write (write to ``.tmp`` then ``os.replace()``) to prevent\n    74→    partial file states.\n    75→\n    76→    Returns the byte count of the stripped preamble (0 when file already starts\n    77→    with ``---`` or no frontmatter delimiter is found).\n    78→    \"\"\"\n    79→    import os\n    80→    import re\n    81→\n    82→    try:\n    83→        content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    84→    except FileNotFoundError:\n    85→        return 0\n    86→\n    87→    # Already starts with frontmatter delimiter -- nothing to strip\n    88→    if content.lstrip().startswith(\"---\"):\n    89→        return 0\n    90→\n    91→    # Search for the first ^--- line\n    92→    match = re.search(r\"^---[ \\t]*$\", content, re.MULTILINE)\n    93→    if match is None:\n    94→        # No frontmatter found at all -- leave file unchanged\n    95→        return 0\n    96→\n    97→    preamble = content[: match.start()]\n    98→    cleaned = content[match.start() :]\n    99→    preamble_bytes = len(preamble.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n   100→\n   101→    # Atomic write: tmp file + os.replace\n   102→    tmp_file = output_file.with_suffix(output_file.suffix + \".tmp\")\n   103→    tmp_file.write_text(cleaned, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   104→    os.replace(tmp_file, output_file)\n   105→\n   106→    _log.info(\"Stripped %d-byte preamble from %s\", preamble_bytes, output_file)\n   107→    return preamble_bytes\n   108→\n   109→\n   110→def _inject_pipeline_diagnostics(\n   111→    output_file: Path,\n   112→    started_at: datetime,\n   113→    finished_at: datetime,\n   114→) -> None:\n   115→    \"\"\"Inject executor-populated pipeline_diagnostics into extraction frontmatter.\n   116→\n   117→    The LLM cannot reliably produce execution timing or environment metadata,\n   118→    so the executor injects these fields post-subprocess (FR-033).\n   119→    \"\"\"\n   120→    content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   121→    if not content.startswith(\"---\"):\n   122→        return\n   123→\n   124→    # Find end of frontmatter\n   125→    end_idx = content.find(\"\\n---\", 3)\n   126→    if end_idx == -1:\n   127→        return\n   128→\n   129→    elapsed = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n   130→    diagnostics_line = (\n   131→        f\"pipeline_diagnostics: \"\n   132→        f\"{{elapsed_seconds: {elapsed:.1f}, \"\n   133→        f\"started_at: \\\"{started_at.isoformat()}\\\", \"\n   134→        f\"finished_at: \\\"{finished_at.isoformat()}\\\"}}\"\n   135→    )\n   136→\n   137→    # Insert before the closing ---\n   138→    new_content = (\n   139→        content[: end_idx]\n   140→        + \"\\n\"\n   141→        + diagnostics_line\n   142→        + content[end_idx:]\n   143→    )\n   144→    output_file.write_text(new_content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   145→\n   146→\n   147→def roadmap_run_step(\n   148→    step: Step,\n   149→    config: PipelineConfig,\n   150→    cancel_check: Callable[[], bool],\n   151→) -> StepResult:\n   152→    \"\"\"Execute a single roadmap step as a Claude subprocess.\n   153→\n   154→    Builds argv with context isolation, launches process, waits with\n   155→    timeout, and returns StepResult.\n   156→    \"\"\"\n   157→    started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   158→\n   159→    if config.dry_run:\n   160→        _log.info(\"[dry-run] Would execute step '%s'\", step.id)\n   161→        return StepResult(\n   162→            step=step,\n   163→            status=StepStatus.PASS,\n   164→            attempt=1,\n   165→            started_at=started_at,\n   166→            finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n   167→        )\n   168→\n   169→    # Inline embedding: read input files into the prompt instead of --file flags\n   170→    embedded = _embed_inputs(step.inputs)\n   171→    if embedded and len(embedded.encode(\"utf-8\")) <= _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT:\n   172→        effective_prompt = step.prompt + \"\\n\\n\" + embedded\n   173→        extra_args: list[str] = []\n   174→    elif embedded:\n   175→        _log.warning(\n   176→            \"Step '%s': embedded inputs exceed %d bytes, falling back to --file flags\",\n   177→            step.id,\n   178→            _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT,\n   179→        )\n   180→        effective_prompt = step.prompt\n   181→        extra_args = [\n   182→            arg\n   183→            for input_path in step.inputs\n   184→            for arg in (\"--file\", str(input_path))\n   185→        ]\n   186→    else:\n   187→        effective_prompt = step.prompt\n   188→        extra_args = []\n   189→\n   190→    proc = ClaudeProcess(\n   191→        prompt=effective_prompt,\n   192→        output_file=step.output_file,\n   193→        error_file=step.output_file.with_suffix(\".err\"),\n   194→        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n   195→        model=step.model or config.model,\n   196→        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n   197→        timeout_seconds=step.timeout_seconds,\n   198→        output_format=\"text\",\n   199→        extra_args=extra_args,\n   200→    )\n   201→\n   202→    proc.start()\n   203→\n   204→    # Poll for cancellation while waiting\n   205→    while proc._process is not None and proc._process.poll() is None:\n   206→        if cancel_check():\n   207→            proc.terminate()\n   208→            return StepResult(\n   209→                step=step,\n   210→                status=StepStatus.CANCELLED,\n   211→                attempt=1,\n   212→                gate_failure_reason=\"Cancelled by external signal\",\n   213→                started_at=started_at,\n   214→                finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n   215→            )\n   216→        time.sleep(1)\n   217→\n   218→    exit_code = proc.wait()\n   219→    finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   220→\n   221→    if exit_code == 124:\n   222→        return StepResult(\n   223→            step=step,\n   224→            status=StepStatus.TIMEOUT,\n   225→            attempt=1,\n   226→            gate_failure_reason=f\"Step '{step.id}' timed out after {step.timeout_seconds}s\",\n   227→            started_at=started_at,\n   228→            finished_at=finished_at,\n   229→        )\n   230→\n   231→    if exit_code != 0:\n   232→        return StepResult(\n   233→            step=step,\n   234→            status=StepStatus.FAIL,\n   235→            attempt=1,\n   236→            gate_failure_reason=f\"Step '{step.id}' exited with code {exit_code}\",\n   237→            started_at=started_at,\n   238→            finished_at=finished_at,\n   239→        )\n   240→\n   241→    # Sanitize output: strip conversational preamble before gate validation\n   242→    _sanitize_output(step.output_file)\n   243→\n   244→    # Inject executor-populated fields into extract step frontmatter (FR-033)\n   245→    if step.id == \"extract\" and step.output_file.exists():\n   246→        _inject_pipeline_diagnostics(step.output_file, started_at, finished_at)\n   247→\n   248→    # Process completed successfully; gate check happens in execute_pipeline\n   249→    return StepResult(\n   250→        step=step,\n   251→        status=StepStatus.PASS,\n   252→        attempt=1,\n   253→        started_at=started_at,\n   254→        finished_at=finished_at,\n   255→    )\n   256→\n   257→\n   258→def _build_steps(config: RoadmapConfig) -> list[Step | list[Step]]:\n   259→    \"\"\"Build the 8-step pipeline with parallel generate group.\n   260→\n   261→    Returns a list where each element is either a single Step (sequential)\n   262→    or a list[Step] (parallel group).\n   263→    \"\"\"\n   264→    out = config.output_dir\n   265→\n   266→    # Agent specs\n   267→    agent_a = config.agents[0]\n   268→    agent_b = config.agents[1] if len(config.agents) > 1 else config.agents[0]\n   269→\n   270→    # Output paths\n   271→    extraction = out / \"extraction.md\"\n   272→    roadmap_a = out / f\"roadmap-{agent_a.id}.md\"\n   273→    roadmap_b = out / f\"roadmap-{agent_b.id}.md\"\n   274→    diff_file = out / \"diff-analysis.md\"\n   275→    debate_file = out / \"debate-transcript.md\"\n   276→    score_file = out / \"base-selection.md\"\n   277→    merge_file = out / \"roadmap.md\"\n   278→    test_strat = out / \"test-strategy.md\"\n   279→\n   280→    steps: list[Step | list[Step]] = [\n   281→        # Step 1: Extract\n   282→        Step(\n   283→            id=\"extract\",\n   284→            prompt=build_extract_prompt(config.spec_file),\n   285→            output_file=extraction,\n   286→            gate=EXTRACT_GATE,\n   287→            timeout_seconds=300,\n   288→            inputs=[config.spec_file],\n   289→            retry_limit=1,\n   290→        ),\n   291→        # Steps 2a+2b: Generate (parallel)\n   292→        [\n   293→            Step(\n   294→                id=f\"generate-{agent_a.id}\",\n   295→                prompt=build_generate_prompt(agent_a, extraction),\n   296→                output_file=roadmap_a,\n   297→                gate=GENERATE_A_GATE,\n   298→                timeout_seconds=900,\n   299→                inputs=[extraction],\n   300→                retry_limit=1,\n   301→                model=agent_a.model,\n   302→            ),\n   303→            Step(\n   304→                id=f\"generate-{agent_b.id}\",\n   305→                prompt=build_generate_prompt(agent_b, extraction),\n   306→                output_file=roadmap_b,\n   307→                gate=GENERATE_B_GATE,\n   308→                timeout_seconds=900,\n   309→                inputs=[extraction],\n   310→                retry_limit=1,\n   311→                model=agent_b.model,\n   312→            ),\n   313→        ],\n   314→        # Step 3: Diff\n   315→        Step(\n   316→            id=\"diff\",\n   317→            prompt=build_diff_prompt(roadmap_a, roadmap_b),\n   318→            output_file=diff_file,\n   319→            gate=DIFF_GATE,\n   320→            timeout_seconds=300,\n   321→            inputs=[roadmap_a, roadmap_b],\n   322→            retry_limit=1,\n   323→        ),\n   324→        # Step 4: Debate\n   325→        Step(\n   326→            id=\"debate\",\n   327→            prompt=build_debate_prompt(diff_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b, config.depth),\n   328→            output_file=debate_file,\n   329→            gate=DEBATE_GATE,\n   330→            timeout_seconds=600,\n   331→            inputs=[diff_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b],\n   332→            retry_limit=1,\n   333→        ),\n   334→        # Step 5: Score\n   335→        Step(\n   336→            id=\"score\",\n   337→            prompt=build_score_prompt(debate_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b),\n   338→            output_file=score_file,\n   339→            gate=SCORE_GATE,\n   340→            timeout_seconds=300,\n   341→            inputs=[debate_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b],\n   342→            retry_limit=1,\n   343→        ),\n   344→        # Step 6: Merge\n   345→        Step(\n   346→            id=\"merge\",\n   347→            prompt=build_merge_prompt(score_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b, debate_file),\n   348→            output_file=merge_file,\n   349→            gate=MERGE_GATE,\n   350→            timeout_seconds=600,\n   351→            inputs=[score_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b, debate_file],\n   352→            retry_limit=1,\n   353→        ),\n   354→        # Step 7: Test Strategy\n   355→        Step(\n   356→            id=\"test-strategy\",\n   357→            prompt=build_test_strategy_prompt(merge_file, extraction),\n   358→            output_file=test_strat,\n   359→            gate=TEST_STRATEGY_GATE,\n   360→            timeout_seconds=300,\n   361→            inputs=[merge_file, extraction],\n   362→            retry_limit=1,\n   363→        ),\n   364→    ]\n   365→\n   366→    return steps\n   367→\n   368→\n   369→def _format_halt_output(results: list[StepResult], config: RoadmapConfig) -> str:\n   370→    \"\"\"Format HALT diagnostic output per spec section 6.2.\"\"\"\n   371→    failed = [r for r in results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)]\n   372→    passed = [r for r in results if r.status == StepStatus.PASS]\n   373→    cancelled = [r for r in results if r.status == StepStatus.CANCELLED]\n   374→\n   375→    if not failed:\n   376→        return \"\"\n   377→\n   378→    fail = failed[-1]\n   379→    step = fail.step\n   380→\n   381→    # Calculate file details if output exists\n   382→    file_info = \"\"\n   383→    if step and step.output_file.exists():\n   384→        content = step.output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   385→        byte_count = len(content.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n   386→        line_count = len(content.splitlines())\n   387→        file_info = f\"  Output size: {byte_count} bytes ({line_count} lines)\\n\"\n   388→    elif step:\n   389→        file_info = \"  Output file: not created\\n\"\n   390→\n   391→    elapsed = f\"{fail.duration_seconds:.0f}s\"\n   392→\n   393→    lines = [\n   394→        f\"ERROR: Roadmap pipeline halted at step '{step.id}' (attempt {fail.attempt}/2)\",\n   395→        f\"  Gate failure: {fail.gate_failure_reason}\",\n   396→        f\"  Output file: {step.output_file}\",\n   397→        file_info.rstrip(),\n   398→        f\"  Step timeout: {step.timeout_seconds}s | Elapsed: {elapsed}\",\n   399→        \"\",\n   400→        f\"Completed steps: {', '.join(f'{r.step.id} (PASS, attempt {r.attempt})' for r in passed) or 'none'}\",\n   401→        f\"Failed step:     {step.id} ({fail.status.value}, attempt {fail.attempt})\",\n   402→    ]\n   403→\n   404→    # Collect skipped steps\n   405→    all_step_ids = _get_all_step_ids(config)\n   406→    executed_ids = {r.step.id for r in results}\n   407→    skipped_ids = [sid for sid in all_step_ids if sid not in executed_ids]\n   408→    if cancelled:\n   409→        skipped_ids = [r.step.id for r in cancelled] + skipped_ids\n   410→\n   411→    if skipped_ids:\n   412→        lines.append(f\"Skipped steps:   {', '.join(skipped_ids)}\")\n   413→\n   414→    lines.extend([\n   415→        \"\",\n   416→        \"To retry from this step:\",\n   417→        f\"  superclaude roadmap run {config.spec_file} --resume\",\n   418→        \"\",\n   419→        \"To inspect the failing output:\",\n   420→        f\"  cat {step.output_file}\",\n   421→    ])\n   422→\n   423→    return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n   424→\n   425→\n   426→def _get_all_step_ids(config: RoadmapConfig) -> list[str]:\n   427→    \"\"\"Get all step IDs in pipeline order.\"\"\"\n   428→    agent_a = config.agents[0]\n   429→    agent_b = config.agents[1] if len(config.agents) > 1 else config.agents[0]\n   430→    return [\n   431→        \"extract\",\n   432→        f\"generate-{agent_a.id}\",\n   433→        f\"generate-{agent_b.id}\",\n   434→        \"diff\",\n   435→        \"debate\",\n   436→        \"score\",\n   437→        \"merge\",\n   438→        \"test-strategy\",\n   439→    ]\n   440→\n   441→\n   442→def _print_step_start(step: Step) -> None:\n   443→    \"\"\"Print progress output when a step starts.\"\"\"\n   444→    print(f\"[roadmap] Starting step: {step.id}\", flush=True)\n   445→\n   446→\n   447→def _print_step_complete(step: Step, result: StepResult) -> None:\n   448→    \"\"\"Print progress output when a step completes.\"\"\"\n   449→    duration = f\"{result.duration_seconds:.0f}s\"\n   450→    if result.status == StepStatus.PASS:\n   451→        print(\n   452→            f\"[roadmap] Step {step.id}  PASS (attempt {result.attempt}, {duration})\",\n   453→            flush=True,\n   454→        )\n   455→    else:\n   456→        print(\n   457→            f\"[roadmap] Step {step.id}  {result.status.value} (attempt {result.attempt}, {duration})\",\n   458→            flush=True,\n   459→        )\n   460→        if result.gate_failure_reason:\n   461→            print(f\"           Reason: {result.gate_failure_reason}\", flush=True)\n   462→\n   463→\n   464→def _dry_run_output(steps: list[Step | list[Step]]) -> None:\n   465→    \"\"\"Print step plan and gate criteria for --dry-run.\"\"\"\n   466→    step_num = 0\n   467→    for entry in steps:\n   468→        if isinstance(entry, list):\n   469→            for s in entry:\n   470→                step_num += 1\n   471→                _print_step_plan(step_num, s, parallel=True)\n   472→        else:\n   473→            step_num += 1\n   474→            _print_step_plan(step_num, entry)\n   475→\n   476→\n   477→def _print_step_plan(num: int, step: Step, parallel: bool = False) -> None:\n   478→    \"\"\"Print a single step's plan entry.\"\"\"\n   479→    par_label = \" (parallel)\" if parallel else \"\"\n   480→    print(f\"Step {num}{par_label}: {step.id}\")\n   481→    print(f\"  Output: {step.output_file}\")\n   482→    print(f\"  Timeout: {step.timeout_seconds}s\")\n   483→    if step.model:\n   484→        print(f\"  Model: {step.model}\")\n   485→    if step.gate:\n   486→        print(f\"  Gate tier: {step.gate.enforcement_tier}\")\n   487→        print(f\"  Gate min_lines: {step.gate.min_lines}\")\n   488→        if step.gate.required_frontmatter_fields:\n   489→            print(f\"  Gate frontmatter: {', '.join(step.gate.required_frontmatter_fields)}\")\n   490→        if step.gate.semantic_checks:\n   491→            checks = [c.name for c in step.gate.semantic_checks]\n   492→            print(f\"  Semantic checks: {', '.join(checks)}\")\n   493→    print()\n   494→\n   495→\n   496→def _save_state(config: RoadmapConfig, results: list[StepResult]) -> None:\n   497→    \"\"\"Write .roadmap-state.json to output_dir via atomic tmp + os.replace().\n   498→\n   499→    Preserves existing ``validation`` key if present (written by\n   500→    ``_save_validation_status``).\n   501→    \"\"\"\n   502→    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n   503→    spec_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n   504→\n   505→    # Preserve existing validation status across state rewrites\n   506→    existing = read_state(state_file)\n   507→    existing_validation = existing.get(\"validation\") if existing else None\n   508→\n   509→    state = {\n   510→        \"schema_version\": 1,\n   511→        \"spec_file\": str(config.spec_file),\n   512→        \"spec_hash\": spec_hash,\n   513→        \"agents\": [{\"model\": a.model, \"persona\": a.persona} for a in config.agents],\n   514→        \"depth\": config.depth,\n   515→        \"last_run\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n   516→        \"steps\": {\n   517→            r.step.id: {\n   518→                \"status\": r.status.value,\n   519→                \"attempt\": r.attempt,\n   520→                \"output_file\": str(r.step.output_file),\n   521→                \"started_at\": r.started_at.isoformat(),\n   522→                \"completed_at\": r.finished_at.isoformat(),\n   523→            }\n   524→            for r in results\n   525→            if r.step\n   526→        },\n   527→    }\n   528→\n   529→    if existing_validation is not None:\n   530→        state[\"validation\"] = existing_validation\n   531→\n   532→    write_state(state, state_file)\n   533→\n   534→\n   535→def write_state(state: dict, path: Path) -> None:\n   536→    \"\"\"Write state dict to path atomically via tmp file + os.replace().\"\"\"\n   537→    import os as _os\n   538→\n   539→    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n   540→    tmp = path.with_suffix(\".tmp\")\n   541→    tmp.write_text(json.dumps(state, indent=2), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   542→    _os.replace(str(tmp), str(path))\n   543→\n   544→\n   545→def read_state(path: Path) -> dict | None:\n   546→    \"\"\"Read state from path with graceful recovery on missing/malformed files.\"\"\"\n   547→    if not path.exists():\n   548→        return None\n   549→    try:\n   550→        text = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   551→        if not text.strip():\n   552→            return None\n   553→        return json.loads(text)\n   554→    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n   555→        return None\n   556→\n   557→\n   558→def apply_decomposition_pass(deliverables: list[Deliverable]) -> list[Deliverable]:\n   559→    \"\"\"Post-generation decomposition pass for the roadmap pipeline.\n   560→\n   561→    Runs after deliverable generation, before output formatting.\n   562→    Splits behavioral deliverables into Implement/Verify pairs.\n   563→\n   564→    Idempotent: running twice produces identical results because\n   565→    already-decomposed deliverables (IDs ending .a/.b) are skipped.\n   566→\n   567→    Preserves milestone-internal ordering: deliverables within each\n   568→    milestone maintain their relative order after decomposition.\n   569→    \"\"\"\n   570→    return decompose_deliverables(deliverables)\n   571→\n   572→\n   573→def execute_roadmap(config: RoadmapConfig, resume: bool = False, no_validate: bool = False) -> None:\n   574→    \"\"\"Execute the roadmap generation pipeline.\n   575→\n   576→    Builds the step list, handles --dry-run and --resume, then\n   577→    delegates to execute_pipeline() with roadmap_run_step.\n   578→\n   579→    After all 8 steps pass, auto-invokes validation unless\n   580→    --no-validate is set or --resume halted on a failed step.\n   581→    \"\"\"\n   582→    config.output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n   583→\n   584→    steps = _build_steps(config)\n   585→\n   586→    # --dry-run: print plan and exit\n   587→    if config.dry_run:\n   588→        _dry_run_output(steps)\n   589→        return\n   590→\n   591→    # --resume: check which steps already pass their gates\n   592→    if resume:\n   593→        from ..pipeline.gates import gate_passed\n   594→\n   595→        steps = _apply_resume(steps, config, gate_passed)\n   596→\n   597→    # Execute pipeline\n   598→    results = execute_pipeline(\n   599→        steps=steps,\n   600→        config=config,\n   601→        run_step=roadmap_run_step,\n   602→        on_step_start=_print_step_start,\n   603→        on_step_complete=_print_step_complete,\n   604→    )\n   605→\n   606→    # Save state\n   607→    _save_state(config, results)\n   608→\n   609→    # Check for failures\n   610→    failures = [r for r in results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)]\n   611→    if failures:\n   612→        halt_msg = _format_halt_output(results, config)\n   613→        print(halt_msg, file=sys.stderr)\n   614→        sys.exit(1)\n   615→\n   616→    print(f\"\\n[roadmap] Pipeline complete: {len(results)} steps passed\", flush=True)\n   617→\n   618→    # Auto-invoke validation after successful pipeline completion\n   619→    if no_validate:\n   620→        print(\"[roadmap] Validation skipped (--no-validate)\", flush=True)\n   621→        _save_validation_status(config, \"skipped\")\n   622→        return\n   623→\n   624→    # Check if validation already completed (--resume path)\n   625→    if resume:\n   626→        state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n   627→        state = read_state(state_file)\n   628→        if state and \"validation\" in state:\n   629→            saved = state[\"validation\"]\n   630→            if saved.get(\"status\") in (\"pass\", \"fail\"):\n   631→                print(\n   632→                    f\"[roadmap] Validation already completed ({saved['status']}), skipping\",\n   633→                    flush=True,\n   634→                )\n   635→                return\n   636→\n   637→    _auto_invoke_validate(config)\n   638→\n   639→\n   640→def _save_validation_status(\n   641→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n   642→    status: str,\n   643→) -> None:\n   644→    \"\"\"Update .roadmap-state.json with validation status.\n   645→\n   646→    Adds or updates the ``validation`` key without modifying existing state.\n   647→\n   648→    Parameters\n   649→    ----------\n   650→    config:\n   651→        RoadmapConfig with output_dir.\n   652→    status:\n   653→        One of \"pass\", \"fail\", or \"skipped\".\n   654→    \"\"\"\n   655→    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n   656→    state = read_state(state_file)\n   657→    if state is None:\n   658→        state = {}\n   659→    state[\"validation\"] = {\n   660→        \"status\": status,\n   661→        \"timestamp\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n   662→    }\n   663→    write_state(state, state_file)\n   664→\n   665→\n   666→def _auto_invoke_validate(config: RoadmapConfig) -> None:\n   667→    \"\"\"Auto-invoke validation after a successful roadmap pipeline run.\n   668→\n   669→    Inherits --model, --max-turns, --debug from the parent roadmap config.\n   670→    Default agent count for roadmap-run invocation is 2 (dual-agent for rigor).\n   671→    \"\"\"\n   672→    from .models import AgentSpec, ValidateConfig\n   673→    from .validate_executor import execute_validate\n   674→\n   675→    # Default to 2 agents for roadmap-run auto-invocation (dual-agent for rigor per OQ-1)\n   676→    validate_agents = config.agents[:2] if len(config.agents) >= 2 else config.agents\n   677→\n   678→    validate_config = ValidateConfig(\n   679→        output_dir=config.output_dir,\n   680→        agents=validate_agents,\n   681→        work_dir=config.output_dir,\n   682→        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n   683→        model=config.model,\n   684→        debug=config.debug,\n   685→    )\n   686→\n   687→    print(\"\\n[roadmap] Auto-invoking validation...\", flush=True)\n   688→\n   689→    try:\n   690→        counts = execute_validate(validate_config)\n   691→        blocking = counts.get(\"blocking_count\", 0)\n   692→        warning = counts.get(\"warning_count\", 0)\n   693→        info = counts.get(\"info_count\", 0)\n   694→        print(\n   695→            f\"[validate] Complete: {blocking} blocking, {warning} warning, {info} info\",\n   696→            flush=True,\n   697→        )\n   698→        validation_status = \"fail\" if blocking > 0 else \"pass\"\n   699→        _save_validation_status(config, validation_status)\n   700→    except FileNotFoundError as exc:\n   701→        _log.error(\"Validation skipped: %s\", exc)\n   702→        print(f\"[validate] Skipped: {exc}\", file=sys.stderr, flush=True)\n   703→        _save_validation_status(config, \"skipped\")\n   704→\n   705→\n   706→def _apply_resume(\n   707→    steps: list[Step | list[Step]],\n   708→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n   709→    gate_fn: Callable,\n   710→) -> list[Step | list[Step]]:\n   711→    \"\"\"Apply --resume logic: skip steps whose outputs already pass gates.\n   712→\n   713→    Also checks for stale spec detection.\n   714→    \"\"\"\n   715→    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n   716→    state = read_state(state_file)\n   717→    force_extract = False\n   718→    if state is not None:\n   719→        saved_hash = state.get(\"spec_hash\", \"\")\n   720→        current_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n   721→        if saved_hash and saved_hash != current_hash:\n   722→            print(\n   723→                f\"WARNING: spec-file has changed since last run.\\n\"\n   724→                f\"  Last hash: {saved_hash[:12]}...\\n\"\n   725→                f\"  Current:   {current_hash[:12]}...\\n\"\n   726→                f\"Forcing re-run of extract step.\",\n   727→                file=sys.stderr,\n   728→                flush=True,\n   729→            )\n   730→            force_extract = True\n   731→\n   732→    skipped = 0\n   733→    result: list[Step | list[Step]] = []\n   734→    found_failure = False\n   735→\n   736→    for entry in steps:\n   737→        if found_failure:\n   738→            # After first failing step, include all remaining steps\n   739→            result.append(entry)\n   740→            continue\n   741→\n   742→        if isinstance(entry, list):\n   743→            # Parallel group: check all steps\n   744→            all_pass = True\n   745→            for s in entry:\n   746→                if s.gate:\n   747→                    passed, _reason = gate_fn(s.output_file, s.gate)\n   748→                    if not passed:\n   749→                        all_pass = False\n   750→                        break\n   751→                else:\n   752→                    all_pass = False\n   753→                    break\n   754→            if all_pass:\n   755→                skipped += len(entry)\n   756→                print(f\"[roadmap] Skipping {', '.join(s.id for s in entry)} (gates pass)\", flush=True)\n   757→            else:\n   758→                found_failure = True\n   759→                result.append(entry)\n   760→        else:\n   761→            # Force re-run of extract on stale spec\n   762→            if force_extract and entry.id == \"extract\":\n   763→                found_failure = True\n   764→                result.append(entry)\n The executor\nseparately appends ``--file <path>`` for each entry in ``step.inputs``.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Literal\n\nfrom .models import AgentSpec\n\n_DEPTH_INSTRUCTIONS = {\n    \"quick\": (\n        \"Conduct a single focused debate round. Each perspective states its \"\n        \"position on the key divergence points, then provide a convergence assessment.\"\n    ),\n    \"standard\": (\n        \"Conduct two debate rounds:\\n\"\n        \"  Round 1: Each perspective states initial positions on divergence points.\\n\"\n        \"  Round 2: Each perspective rebuts the other's key claims.\\n\"\n        \"Then provide a convergence assessment.\"\n    ),\n    \"deep\": (\n        \"Conduct three debate rounds:\\n\"\n        \"  Round 1: Each perspective states initial positions on divergence points.\\n\"\n        \"  Round 2: Each perspective rebuts the other's key claims.\\n\"\n        \"  Round 3: Final synthesis -- each perspective identifies concessions and \"\n        \"remaining disagreements.\\n\"\n        \"Then frontmatter.\\n\"\n    \"\\n\"\n    \"Correct start:\\n\"\n    \"---\\n\"\n    \"key: value\\n\"\n    \"---\\n\"\n    \"\\n\"\n    \"Incorrect start:\\n\"\n    \"Here is the output:\\n\"\n    \"---\\n\"\n    \"key: value\\n\"\n    \"---\\n\"\n    \"</output_format>\"\n)\n\n\ndef build_extract_prompt(spec_file: Path) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Prompt for step 'extract'.\n\n    Instructs Claude to \"You are a requirements extraction specialist.\\n\\n\"\n        \"Read the provided specification file and produce a requirements extraction document.\\n\\n\"\n        \"Your output MUST begin with
