external dependencies\\n\"\n        \"- success_criteria_count: (integer) count of measurable success criteria\\n\"\n        \"- extraction_mode: (string) one of: full, partial, incremental\\n\\n\"\n        \"After the frontmatter, provide the following 8 structured sections:\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Functional Requirements\\n\"\n        \"Numbered list with FR-NNN IDs (e.g. FR-001, FR-002). \"\n        \"Extract every functional requirement, even implicit ones.\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Non-Functional Requirements\\n\"\n        \"Numbered list with NFR-NNN IDs (e.g. NFR-001, NFR-002). \"\n        \"Include performance, security, scalability, maintainability.\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Complexity Assessment\\n\"\n        \"Provide complexity_score and complexity_class with detailed scoring rationale.\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Architectural Constraints\\n\"\n        \"List all architectural constraints, technology mandates, and integration boundaries.\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Risk Inventory\\n\"\n        \"Numbered list of identified risks with severity (high/medium/low) and mitigation.\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Dependency Inventory\\n\"\n        \"List all external dependencies, libraries, services, and integration points.\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Success Criteria\\n\"\n        \"Measurable success criteria with acceptance thresholds.\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Open Questions\\n\"\n        \"Ambiguities, gaps, or items requiring stakeholder clarification.\\n\\n\"\n        \"Be thorough and precise. Extract every requirement, even implicit ones.\"\n    ) + _OUTPUT_FORMAT_BLOCK\n\n\ndef build_generate_prompt(agent: AgentSpec, extraction_path: Path) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Prompt for step 'generate-{agent.id}'.\n\n    Instructs Claude to read the extraction document and generate a\n    complete project roadmap with the agent's persona as a role instruction.\n    References expanded extraction fields for richer context.\n    \"\"\"\n    return (\n        f\"You are a {agent.persona} specialist creating a project roadmap.\\n\\n\"\n        \"Read the provided requirements extraction document and generate a comprehensive \"\n        \"project roadmap.\\n\\n\"\n        \"The extraction document contains YAML frontmatter with these fields you should \"\n        \"reference for context:\\n\"\n        \"- spec_source, generated, generator: provenance metadata\\n\"\n        \"- functional_requirements, nonfunctional_requirements, total_requirements: scope counts\\n\"\n        \"- complexity_score, complexity_class: complexity assessment\\n\"\n        \"- domains_detected: number of technical domains to address\\n\"\n        \"- risks_identified: number of risks to mitigate in the roadmap\\n\"\n        \"- dependencies_identified: external dependencies to plan around\\n\"\n        \"- success_criteria_count: measurable criteria to validate against\\n\"\n        \"- extraction_mode: extraction completeness indicator\\n\\n\"\n        \"Your output MUST begin with YAML frontmatter delimited by --- lines containing:\\n\"\n        f\"- spec_source: (the source specification filename)\\n\"\n        \"- complexity_score: (float 0.0-1.0 from the extraction)\\n\"\n        f\"- primary_persona: {agent.persona}\\n\\n\"\n        \"After the frontmatter, provide a complete roadmap including:\\n\"\n        \"1. Executive summary\\n\"\n        \"2. Phased implementation plan with milestones\\n\"\n        \"3. Risk assessment and mitigation strategies\\n\"\n        \"4. Resource requirements and dependencies\\n\"\n        \"5. Success criteria and validation approach\\n\"\n        \"6. Timeline estimates per phase\\n\\n\"\n        f\"Apply your {agent.persona} perspective throughout: prioritize concerns, \"\n        f\"risks, and recommendations that a {agent.persona} would emphasize.\\n\\n\"\n        \"Use numbered and bulleted lists for actionable items. Be specific and concrete.\"\n    ) + _OUTPUT_FORMAT_BLOCK\n\n\ndef build_diff_prompt(variant_a_path: Path, variant_b_path: Path) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Prompt for step 'diff'.\n\n    Instructs Claude to compare two roadmap variants and produce\n    diff-analysis.md with frontmatter fields total_diff_points,\n    shared_assumptions_count.\n    \"\"\"\n    return (\n        \"You are a comparative analysis specialist.\\n\\n\"\n        \"Read the two provided roadmap variants and produce a structured diff analysis.\\n\\n\"\n        \"Your output MUST begin with YAML frontmatter delimited by --- lines containing:\\n\"\n        \"- total_diff_points: (integer count of identified divergence points)\\n\"\n        \"- shared_assumptions_count: (integer count of shared assumptions)\\n\\n\"\n        \"After the frontmatter, provide:\\n\"\n        \"1. Shared assumptions and agreements between variants\\n\"\n        \"2. Numbered list of divergence points, each with:\\n\"\n        \"   - Description of the difference\\n\"\n        \"   - Which variant takes which position\\n\"\n        \"   - Potential impact of each approach\\n\"\n        \"3. Areas where one variant is clearly stronger\\n\"\n        \"4. Areas requiring debate to resolve\\n\\n\"\n        \"Be objective. Present both positions fairly without bias.\"\n    ) + _OUTPUT_FORMAT_BLOCK\n\n\ndef build_debate_prompt(\n    diff_path: Path,\n    variant_a_path: Path,\n    variant_b_path: Path,\n    depth: Literal[\"quick\", \"standard\", \"deep\"],\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Prompt for step 'debate'.\n\n    Depth controls the number of debate rounds embedded in the prompt.\n    \"\"\"\n    depth_instruction = _DEPTH_INSTRUCTIONS[depth]\n    return (\n        \"You are a structured debate facilitator.\\n\\n\"\n        \"Read the provided diff analysis and both roadmap variants. \"\n        \"Facilitate a structured adversarial debate between the two approaches.\\n\\n\"\n        f\"Debate format:\\n{depth_instruction}\\n\\n\"\n        \"Your output MUST begin with YAML frontmatter delimited by --- lines containing:\\n\"\n        \"- convergence_score: (float 0.0-1.0 indicating how much agreement was reached)\\n\"\n        \"- rounds_completed: (integer number of debate rounds conducted)\\n\\n\"\n        \"After the frontmatter, provide the full debate transcript with:\\n\"\n        \"- Each round clearly labeled\\n\"\n        \"- Positions attributed to Variant A and Variant B\\n\"\n        \"- A convergence assessment summarizing areas of agreement and remaining disputes\\n\\n\"\n        \"Ensure each perspective argues its strongest case. Do not artificially force agreement.\"\n    ) + _OUTPUT_FORMAT_BLOCK\n\n\ndef build_score_prompt(\n    debate_path: Path,\n    variant_a_path: Path,\n    variant_b_path: Path,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Prompt for step 'score'.\n\n    Instructs Claude to select a base variant and score both.\n    \"\"\"\n    return (\n        \"You are an objective evaluation specialist.\\n\\n\"\n        \"Read the debate transcript and both roadmap variants. \"\n        \"Score each variant and select a base for the final merge.\\n\\n\"\n        \"Your output MUST begin with YAML frontmatter delimited by --- lines containing:\\n\"\n        \"- base_variant: (string: the identifier of the selected base variant)\\n\"\n        \"- variant_scores: (string summary of scores, e.g. 'A:78 B:72')\\n\\n\"\n        \"After the frontmatter, provide:\\n\"\n        \"1. Scoring criteria used (derived from the debate)\\n\"\n        \"2. Per-criterion scores for each variant\\n\"\n        \"3. Overall scores with justification\\n\"\n        \"4. Base variant selection rationale\\n\"\n        \"5. Specific improvements from the non-base variant to incorporate in merge\\n\\n\"\n        \"Be evidence-based. Reference specific debate points and variant content.\"\n    ) + _OUTPUT_FORMAT_BLOCK\n\n\ndef build_merge_prompt(\n    base_selection_path: Path,\n    variant_a_path: Path,\n    variant_b_path: Path,\n    debate_path: Path,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Prompt for step 'merge'.\n\n    Instructs Claude to produce the final merged roadmap.\n    \"\"\"\n    return (\n        \"You are a synthesis specialist producing the final merged roadmap.\\n\\n\"\n        \"Read the base selection document, both roadmap variants, and the debate transcript. \"\n        \"Produce a final merged roadmap that uses the selected base variant as foundation \"\n        \"and incorporates the best elements from the other variant as identified in the debate.\\n\\n\"\n        \"Your output MUST begin with YAML frontmatter delimited by --- lines containing:\\n\"\n        \"- spec_source: (the original specification filename)\\n\"\n        \"- complexity_score: (float from the extraction)\\n\"\n        \"- adversarial: true\\n\\n\"\n        \"After the frontmatter, provide the complete final roadmap with:\\n\"\n        \"1. Executive summary (synthesized from both variants)\\n\"\n        \"2. Phased implementation plan incorporating debate-resolved improvements\\n\"\n        \"3. Risk assessment merging insights from both perspectives\\n\"\n        \"4. Resource requirements\\n\"\n        \"5. Success criteria and validation approach\\n\"\n        \"6. Timeline estimates\\n\\n\"\n        \"Use proper heading hierarchy (H2, H3, H4) with no gaps. \"\n        \"Ensure all internal cross-references resolve. \"\n        \"Do not duplicate heading text at H2 or H3 level.\"\n    ) + _OUTPUT_FORMAT_BLOCK\n\n\ndef build_test_strategy_prompt(\n    roadmap_path: Path,\n    extraction_path: Path,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Prompt for step 'test-strategy'.\n\n    Instructs Claude to produce a test strategy for the roadmap.\n    \"\"\"\n    return (\n        \"You are a test strategy specialist.\\n\\n\"\n        \"Read the final roadmap and the requirements extraction document. \"\n        \"Produce a comprehensive test strategy.\\n\\n\"\n        \"Your output MUST begin with YAML frontmatter delimited by --- lines containing:\\n\"\n        \"- validation_milestones: (integer count of validation milestones defined)\\n\"\n        \"- interleave_ratio: (string describing test-to-implementation ratio, e.g. '1:3')\\n\\n\"\n        \"After the frontmatter, provide:\\n\"\n        \"1. Validation milestones mapped to roadmap phases\\n\"\n        \"2. Test categories (unit, integration, E2E, acceptance)\\n\"\n        \"3. Test-implementation interleaving strategy\\n\"\n        \"4. Risk-based test prioritization\\n\"\n        \"5. Acceptance criteria per milestone\\n\"\n        \"6. Quality gates between phases\\n\\n\"\n        \"Be specific about what to test at each milestone.\"\n    ) + _OUTPUT_FORMAT_BLOCK\n```\n\n## Key function signatures and patterns\n\n### Function signatures\n- `build_extract_prompt(spec_file: Path) -> str`\n- `build_generate_prompt(agent: AgentSpec, extraction_path: Path) -> str`\n- `build_diff_prompt(variant_a_path: Path, variant_b_path: Path) -> str`\n- `build_debate_prompt(diff_path: Path, variant_a_path: Path, variant_b_path: Path, depth: Literal[\"quick\", \"standard\", \"deep\"]) -> str`\n- `build_score_prompt(debate_path: Path, variant_a_path: Path, variant_b_path: Path) -> str`\n- `build_merge_prompt(base_selection_path: Path, variant_a_path: Path, variant_b_path: Path, debate_path: Path) -> str`\n- `build_test_strategy_prompt(roadmap_path: Path, extraction_path: Path) -> str`\n\n### Structural prompt patterns\nThis module is very consistent. Each prompt builder:\n1. starts with a role sentence: `\"You are a ... specialist\"`\n2. instructs the model to read provided input file(s)\n3. requires YAML frontmatter first\n4. specifies exact required frontmatter fields\n5. specifies exact output body sections\n6. appends the shared `_OUTPUT_FORMAT_BLOCK`\n\n### Important prompt conventions\n- Prompt builders are pure string builders.\n- They do not inspect file contents.\n- Even though file path parameters are passed in, most prompts don’t interpolate those paths into the string; the executor separately provides files via inline embedding or `--file`.\n- `_OUTPUT_FORMAT_BLOCK` is reused to enforce no conversational preamble before YAML.\n\n### Debate depth pattern\n`_DEPTH_INSTRUCTIONS` is a simple mapping:\n- `quick` → 1 round\n- `standard` → 2 rounds\n- `deep` → 3 rounds\n\nThat is the main prompt-level variability currently present.\n\n---\n\n# 2) `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/roadmap/gates.py`\n\n## Full file contents\n\n```python\n\"\"\"Roadmap gate criteria -- data definitions for each pipeline step's gate.\n\nThis module defines GateCriteria instances as module-level constants.\nGate criteria are pure data -- no logic, no imports from pipeline/gates.py\nenforcement code (NFR-005).\n\nSemantic check functions are defined here as pure functions accepting\nfile content and returning bool. They are registered on the STRICT-tier\nGateCriteria instances.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom ..pipeline.models import GateCriteria, SemanticCheck\n\n\n# --- Semantic check functions (pure: content -> bool) ---\n\ndef _no_heading_gaps(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Verify heading levels increment by at most 1 (no H2 -> H4 skip).\"\"\"\n    prev_level = 0\n    for line in content.splitlines():\n        stripped = line.lstrip()\n        if stripped.startswith(\"#\"):\n            level = 0\n            for ch in stripped:\n                if ch == \"#\":\n                    level += 1\n                else:\n                    break\n            if prev_level > 0 and level > prev_level + 1:\n                return False\n            prev_level = level\n    return True\n\n\ndef _cross_refs_resolve(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Verify all 'See section' / cross-reference patterns have matching headings.\n\n    Checks that internal references like 'See section X.Y' or 'See X.Y'\n    have corresponding headings. Unresolved references emit warnings but\n    do not block the pipeline (warning-only mode per OQ-001 for v2.20).\n    \"\"\"\n    import re\n    import warnings\n\n    # Extract heading anchors (simplified: heading text, lowercased)\n    headings: set[str] = set()\n    for line in content.splitlines():\n        stripped = line.lstrip()\n        if stripped.startswith(\"#\"):\n            heading_text = stripped.lstrip(\"#\").strip().lower()\n            headings.add(heading_text)\n\n    # Find cross-references like \"See section X\" or \"(see X.Y)\"\n    refs = re.findall(r'[Ss]ee\\s+(?:[Ss]ection\\s+)?[\"\\']?(\\d+(?:\\.\\d+)*)', content)\n    # If there are no cross-references, that's fine\n    if not refs:\n        return True\n\n    # Check each reference against headings\n    unresolved: list[str] = []\n    for ref in refs:\n        found = any(ref in h for h in headings)\n        if not found:\n            # Also check if the section number appears as a heading prefix\n            found = any(h.startswith(ref) for h in headings)\n        if not found:\n            unresolved.append(ref)\n\n    if unresolved:\n        for ref in unresolved:\n            warnings.warn(\n                f\"Unresolved cross-reference: 'See section {ref}' has no matching heading\",\n                stacklevel=2,\n            )\n        # Warning-only mode (OQ-001): return True to avoid blocking pipeline\n        return True\n\n    return True\n\n\ndef _no_duplicate_headings(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"No duplicate H2 or H3 heading text.\"\"\"\n    seen: dict[int, set[str]] = {2: set(), 3: set()}\n    for line in content.splitlines():\n        stripped = line.lstrip()\n        if stripped.startswith(\"## \") and not stripped.startswith(\"### \"):\n            text = stripped[3:].strip().lower()\n            if text in seen[2]:\n                return False\n            seen[2].add(text)\n        elif stripped.startswith(\"### \"):\n            text = stripped[4:].strip().lower()\n            if text in seen[3]:\n                return False\n            seen[3].add(text)\n    return True\n\n\ndef _frontmatter_values_non_empty(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"All YAML frontmatter fields have non-empty values.\"\"\"\n    stripped = content.lstrip()\n    if not stripped.startswith(\"---\"):\n        return False\n\n    rest = stripped[3:].lstrip(\"\\n\")\n    end_idx = rest.find(\"\\n---\")\n    if end_idx == -1:\n        return False\n\n    frontmatter_text = rest[:end_idx]\n    for line in frontmatter_text.splitlines():\n        line = line.strip()\n        if \":\" in line:\n            _key, value = line.split(\":\", 1)\n            if not value.strip():\n                return False\n    return True\n\n\ndef _has_actionable_content(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"At least one section contains numbered or bulleted items.\"\"\"\n    import re\n    # Look for markdown list items: \"- \", \"* \", \"1. \", \"2. \", etc.\n    return bool(re.search(r'^\\s*(?:[-*]|\\d+\\.)\\s+\\S', content, re.MULTILINE))\n\n\ndef _parse_frontmatter(content: str) -> dict[str, str] | None:\n    \"\"\"Extract YAML frontmatter key-value pairs from content.\n\n    Returns a dict of key→value strings, or None if no frontmatter found.\n    \"\"\"\n    stripped = content.lstrip()\n    if not stripped.startswith(\"---\"):\n        return None\n\n    rest = stripped[3:].lstrip(\"\\n\")\n    end_idx = rest.find(\"\\n---\")\n    if end_idx == -1:\n        return None\n\n    result: dict[str, str] = {}\n    for line in rest[:end_idx].splitlines():\n        line = line.strip()\n        if \":\" in line:\n            key, value = line.split(\":\", 1)\n            result[key.strip()] = value.strip()\n    return result\n\n\ndef _high_severity_count_zero(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Validate that high_severity_count equals zero in fidelity report frontmatter.\n\n    Returns True only if the frontmatter contains high_severity_count with\n    integer value 0. Returns False if:\n    - Frontmatter is missing\n    - high_severity_count field is missing\n    - high_severity_count > 0\n\n    Raises TypeError if high_severity_count value is not parseable as an integer.\n    \"\"\"\n    fm = _parse_frontmatter(content)\n    if fm is None:\n        return False\n\n    value = fm.get(\"high_severity_count\")\n    if value is None:\n        return False\n\n    try:\n        count = int(value)\n    except (ValueError, TypeError):\n        raise TypeError(\n            f\"high_severity_count must be an integer, got: {value!r}\"\n        )\n\n    return count == 0\n\n\ndef _tasklist_ready_consistent(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Validate tasklist_ready is consistent with severity counts.\n\n    Consistency rule: tasklist_ready=true requires high_severity_count=0\n    AND validation_complete=true. If tasklist_ready=true but either\n    condition fails, this returns False (inconsistency detected).\n\n    Returns False if:\n    - Frontmatter is missing\n    - tasklist_ready field is missing\n    - Inconsistency: tasklist_ready=true but high_severity_count > 0\n    - Inconsistency: tasklist_ready=true but validation_complete != true\n    \"\"\"\n    fm = _parse_frontmatter(content)\n    if fm is None:\n        return False\n\n    tasklist_ready_str = fm.get(\"tasklist_ready\")\n    if tasklist_ready_str is None:\n        return False\n\n    tasklist_ready = tasklist_ready_str.lower() == \"true\"\n\n    if not tasklist_ready:\n        # If tasklist_ready is false, that's always consistent\n        return True\n\n    # tasklist_ready is true -- verify consistency\n    high_str = fm.get(\"high_severity_count\")\n    if high_str is None:\n        return False\n    try:\n        high_count = int(high_str)\n    except (ValueError, TypeError):\n        return False\n\n    if high_count > 0:\n        return False\n\n    validation_str = fm.get(\"validation_complete\")\n    if validation_str is None:\n        return False\n    if validation_str.lower() != \"true\":\n        return False\n\n    return True\n\n\ndef _convergence_score_valid(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"convergence_score frontmatter value parses as float in [0.0, 1.0].\"\"\"\n    stripped = content.lstrip()\n    if not stripped.startswith(\"---\"):\n        return False\n\n    rest = stripped[3:].lstrip(\"\\n\")\n    end_idx = rest.find(\"\\n---\")\n    if end_idx == -1:\n        return False\n\n    frontmatter_text = rest[:end_idx]\n    for line in frontmatter_text.splitlines():\n        line = line.strip()\n        if line.startswith(\"convergence_score:\"):\n            value = line.split(\":\", 1)[1].strip()\n            try:\n                score = float(value)\n                return 0.0 <= score <= 1.0\n            except ValueError:\n                return False\n    return False  # convergence_score field not found\n\n\n# --- GateCriteria instances ---\n\nEXTRACT_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[\n        \"spec_source\",\n        \"generated\",\n        \"generator\",\n        \"functional_requirements\",\n        \"nonfunctional_requirements\",\n        \"total_requirements\",\n        \"complexity_score\",\n        \"complexity_class\",\n        \"domains_detected\",\n        \"risks_identified\",\n        \"dependencies_identified\",\n        \"success_criteria_count\",\n        \"extraction_mode\",\n    ],\n    min_lines=50,\n    enforcement_tier=\"STRICT\",\n)\n\nGENERATE_A_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[\"spec_source\", \"complexity_score\", \"primary_persona\"],\n    min_lines=100,\n    enforcement_tier=\"STRICT\",\n    semantic_checks=[\n        SemanticCheck(\n            name=\"frontmatter_values_non_empty\",\n            check_fn=_frontmatter_values_non_empty,\n            failure_message=\"One or more required frontmatter fields have empty values\",\n        ),\n        SemanticCheck(\n            name=\"has_actionable_content\",\n            check_fn=_has_actionable_content,\n            failure_message=\"No numbered or bulleted items found -- roadmap must contain actionable content\",\n        ),\n    ],\n)\n\nGENERATE_B_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[\"spec_source\", \"complexity_score\", \"primary_persona\"],\n    min_lines=100,\n    enforcement_tier=\"STRICT\",\n    semantic_checks=[\n        SemanticCheck(\n            name=\"frontmatter_values_non_empty\",\n            check_fn=_frontmatter_values_non_empty,\n            failure_message=\"One or more required frontmatter fields have empty values\",\n        ),\n        SemanticCheck(\n            name=\"has_actionable_content\",\n            check_fn=_has_actionable_content,\n            failure_message=\"No numbered or bulleted items found -- roadmap must contain actionable content\",\n        ),\n    ],\n)\n\nDIFF_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[\"total_diff_points\", \"shared_assumptions_count\"],\n    min_lines=30,\n    enforcement_tier=\"STANDARD\",\n)\n\nDEBATE_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[\"convergence_score\", \"rounds_completed\"],\n    min_lines=50,\n    enforcement_tier=\"STRICT\",\n    semantic_checks=[\n        SemanticCheck(\n            name=\"convergence_score_valid\",\n            check_fn=_convergence_score_valid,\n            failure_message=\"convergence_score must be a float in [0.0, 1.0]\",\n        ),\n    ],\n)\n\nSCORE_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[\"base_variant\", \"variant_scores\"],\n    min_lines=20,\n    enforcement_tier=\"STANDARD\",\n)\n\nMERGE_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[\"spec_source\", \"complexity_score\", \"adversarial\"],\n    min_lines=150,\n    enforcement_tier=\"STRICT\",\n    semantic_checks=[\n        SemanticCheck(\n            name=\"no_heading_gaps\",\n            check_fn=_no_heading_gaps,\n            failure_message=\"Heading level gap detected (e.g. H2 -> H4 without H3)\",\n        ),\n        SemanticCheck(\n            name=\"cross_refs_resolve\",\n            check_fn=_cross_refs_resolve,\n            failure_message=\"Internal cross-reference does not resolve to an existing heading\",\n        ),\n        SemanticCheck(\n            name=\"no_duplicate_headings\",\n            check_fn=_no_duplicate_headings,\n            failure_message=\"Duplicate H2 or H3 heading text detected\",\n        ),\n    ],\n)\n\nTEST_STRATEGY_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[\"validation_milestones\", \"interleave_ratio\"],\n    min_lines=40,\n    enforcement_tier=\"STANDARD\",\n)\n\n# All gates in pipeline order for reference\nALL_GATES = [\n    (\"extract\", EXTRACT_GATE),\n    (\"generate-A\", GENERATE_A_GATE),\n    (\"generate-B\", GENERATE_B_GATE),\n    (\"diff\", DIFF_GATE),\n    (\"debate\", DEBATE_GATE),\n    (\"score\", SCORE_GATE),\n    (\"merge\", MERGE_GATE),\n    (\"test-strategy\", TEST_STRATEGY_GATE),\n]\n```\n\n## Key function signatures and patterns\n\n### Semantic check signatures\nAll semantic checks follow:\n- `fn(content: str) -> bool`\n\nHelpers:\n- `_no_heading_gaps(content: str) -> bool`\n- `_cross_refs_resolve(content: str) -> bool`\n- `_no_duplicate_headings(content: str) -> bool`\n- `_frontmatter_values_non_empty(content: str) -> bool`\n- `_has_actionable_content(content: str) -> bool`\n- `_parse_frontmatter(content: str) -> dict[str, str] | None`\n- `_high_severity_count_zero(content: str) -> bool`\n- `_tasklist_ready_consistent(content: str) -> bool`\n- `_convergence_score_valid(content: str) -> bool`\n\n## How gates are defined and registered\n\n### Definition pattern\nEach gate is a module-level constant:\n- `EXTRACT_GATE = GateCriteria(...)`\n- `GENERATE_A_GATE = GateCriteria(...)`\n- etc.\n\nA gate consists of:\n- `required_frontmatter_fields=[...]`\n- `min_lines=<int>`\n- `enforcement_tier=\"STRICT\" | \"STANDARD\"`\n- optional `semantic_checks=[SemanticCheck(...), ...]`\n\n### Semantic registration pattern\nSemantic checks are attached inline inside `GateCriteria(...)`:\n\n```python\nsemantic_checks=[\n    SemanticCheck(\n        name=\"frontmatter_values_non_empty\",\n        check_fn=_frontmatter_values_non_empty,\n        failure_message=\"One or more required frontmatter fields have empty values\",\n    ),\n]\n```\n\nSo “registration” is declarative, via object construction, not via a central registry function.\n\n### Pipeline-order registration\nThe canonical ordered list is:\n\n```python\nALL_GATES = [\n    (\"extract\", EXTRACT_GATE),\n    (\"generate-A\", GENERATE_A_GATE),\n    (\"generate-B\", GENERATE_B_GATE),\n    (\"diff\", DIFF_GATE),\n    (\"debate\", DEBATE_GATE),\n    (\"score\", SCORE_GATE),\n    (\"merge\", MERGE_GATE),\n    (\"test-strategy\", TEST_STRATEGY_GATE),\n]\n```\n\nThat is the closest thing to a global roadmap gate registry.\n\n## Gate patterns worth noting for Phase 3\n\n### Tier split\n- STRICT: `extract`, `generate-A`, `generate-B`, `debate`, `merge`\n- STANDARD: `diff`, `score`, `test-strategy`\n\n### Common patterns\n- Extract gate only checks required frontmatter + min lines.\n- Generate gates add:\n  - non-empty frontmatter values\n  - actionable content\n- Debate gate adds convergence score validation.\n- Merge gate is the strongest structural gate:\n  - no heading gaps\n  - cross refs resolve\n  - no duplicate headings\n\n### Special note\n`_high_severity_count_zero` and `_tasklist_ready_consistent` exist here but are not currently attached to any `GateCriteria` in this file.\n\n---\n\n# 3) `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/roadmap/validate_gates.py`\n\n## Full file contents\n\n```python\n\"\"\"Validation gate criteria -- data definitions for validate pipeline steps.\n\nDefines GateCriteria instances for the validation subsystem:\nREFLECT_GATE and ADVERSARIAL_MERGE_GATE.\n\nImports from roadmap/gates.py only (unidirectional dependency preserved).\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom ..pipeline.models import GateCriteria, SemanticCheck\nfrom .gates import _frontmatter_values_non_empty\n\n\ndef _has_agreement_table(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check that content contains a markdown agreement table.\n\n    An agreement table has a header row containing 'agree' or 'agreement'\n    (case-insensitive) and at least one pipe-delimited data row.\n    \"\"\"\n    lines = content.splitlines()\n    for i, line in enumerate(lines):\n        if \"|\" in line and (\"agree\" in line.lower() or \"agreement\" in line.lower()):\n            # Check for a separator row after header\n            if i + 1 < len(lines) and \"|\" in lines[i + 1] and \"-\" in lines[i + 1]:\n                return True\n    return False\n\n\nREFLECT_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[\n        \"blocking_issues_count\",\n        \"warnings_count\",\n        \"tasklist_ready\",\n    ],\n    min_lines=20,\n    enforcement_tier=\"STRICT\",\n    semantic_checks=[\n        SemanticCheck(\n            name=\"frontmatter_values_non_empty\",\n            check_fn=_frontmatter_values_non_empty,\n            failure_message=\"One or more required frontmatter fields have empty values\",\n        ),\n    ],\n)\n\nADVERSARIAL_MERGE_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[\n        \"blocking_issues_count\",\n        \"warnings_count\",\n        \"tasklist_ready\",\n        \"validation_mode\",\n        \"validation_agents\",\n    ],\n    min_lines=30,\n    enforcement_tier=\"STRICT\",\n    semantic_checks=[\n        SemanticCheck(\n            name=\"frontmatter_values_non_empty\",\n            check_fn=_frontmatter_values_non_empty,\n            failure_message=\"One or more required frontmatter fields have empty values\",\n        ),\n        SemanticCheck(\n            name=\"agreement_table_present\",\n            check_fn=_has_agreement_table,\n            failure_message=\"No agreement table found -- adversarial merge requires an agreement/disagreement table\",\n        ),\n    ],\n)\n```\n\n## Key function signatures and patterns\n\n- `_has_agreement_table(content: str) -> bool`\n\n## Validate gate patterns\n\n### Imports\nThis module intentionally reuses `_frontmatter_values_non_empty` from `roadmap.gates`, preserving a one-way dependency.\n\n### Gates defined\n- `REFLECT_GATE`\n- `ADVERSARIAL_MERGE_GATE`\n\n### Registration pattern\nSame as `gates.py`: module-level `GateCriteria(...)` constants with inline `SemanticCheck(...)`.\n\n## Important Phase 3 observation\nIf you expected `_high_severity_count_zero` and `_tasklist_ready_consistent` here, they are not here yet. This may indicate:\n- either Phase 3 plans to move or reuse them here\n- or validation/fidelity integration is incomplete and those checks have only been added to `gates.py` so far\n\n---\n\n# 4) `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/roadmap/executor.py`\n\n## Full file contents\n\n```python\n\"\"\"Roadmap executor -- orchestrates the 8-step roadmap pipeline.\n\nBuilds the step list with parallel generate group, defines\n``roadmap_run_step`` as the StepRunner, and delegates to\n``execute_pipeline()`` from the pipeline module.\n\nContext isolation: each subprocess receives only its prompt and --file inputs.\nNo --continue, --session, or --resume flags are passed (FR-003, FR-023).\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport hashlib\nimport json\nimport logging\nimport sys\nimport time\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Callable\n\nfrom ..pipeline.deliverables import decompose_deliverables\nfrom ..pipeline.executor import execute_pipeline\nfrom ..pipeline.models import Deliverable, PipelineConfig, Step, StepResult, StepStatus\nfrom ..pipeline.process import ClaudeProcess\nfrom .gates import (\n    DEBATE_GATE,\n    DIFF_GATE,\n    EXTRACT_GATE,\n    GENERATE_A_GATE,\n    GENERATE_B_GATE,\n    MERGE_GATE,\n    SCORE_GATE,\n    TEST_STRATEGY_GATE,\n)\nfrom .models import RoadmapConfig\nfrom .prompts import (\n    build_debate_prompt,\n    build_diff_prompt,\n    build_extract_prompt,\n    build_generate_prompt,\n    build_merge_prompt,\n    build_score_prompt,\n    build_test_strategy_prompt,\n)\n\n_log = logging.getLogger(\"superclaude.roadmap.executor\")\n\n# Threshold above which inline embedding falls back to --file flags\n_EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT = 100 * 1024  # 100 KB\n\n\ndef _embed_inputs(input_paths: list[Path]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Read input files and return their contents as fenced code blocks.\n\n    Each file is wrapped in a fenced block with a ``# <path>`` header.\n    Returns an empty string when *input_paths* is empty (no-op).\n    \"\"\"\n    if not input_paths:\n        return \"\"\n\n    blocks: list[str] = []\n    for p in input_paths:\n        content = Path(p).read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        blocks.append(f\"# {p}\\n```\\n{content}\\n```\")\n    return \"\\n\\n\".join(blocks)\n\n\ndef _sanitize_output(output_file: Path) -> int:\n    \"\"\"Strip conversational preamble before YAML frontmatter in a step output file.\n\n    Reads the file, finds the first ``^---`` line, and removes everything before\n    it.  Uses atomic write (write to ``.tmp`` then ``os.replace()``) to prevent\n    partial file states.\n\n    Returns the byte count of the stripped preamble (0 when file already starts\n    with ``---`` or no frontmatter delimiter is found).\n    \"\"\"\n    import os\n    import re\n\n    try:\n        content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        return 0\n\n    # Already starts with frontmatter delimiter -- nothing to strip\n    if content.lstrip().startswith(\"---\"):\n        return 0\n\n    # Search for the first ^--- line\n    match = re.search(r\"^---[ \\t]*$\", content, re.MULTILINE)\n    if match is None:\n        # No frontmatter found at all -- leave file unchanged\n        return 0\n\n    preamble = content[: match.start()]\n    cleaned = content[match.start() :]\n    preamble_bytes = len(preamble.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n\n    # Atomic write: tmp file + os.replace\n    tmp_file = output_file.with_suffix(output_file.suffix + \".tmp\")\n    tmp_file.write_text(cleaned, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    os.replace(tmp_file, output_file)\n\n    _log.info(\"Stripped %d-byte preamble from %s\", preamble_bytes, output_file)\n    return preamble_bytes\n\n\ndef _inject_pipeline_diagnostics(\n    output_file: Path,\n    started_at: datetime,\n    finished_at: datetime,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Inject executor-populated pipeline_diagnostics into extraction frontmatter.\n\n    The LLM cannot reliably produce execution timing or environment metadata,\n    so the executor injects these fields post-subprocess (FR-033).\n    \"\"\"\n    content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    if not content.startswith(\"---\"):\n        return\n\n    # Find end of frontmatter\n    end_idx = content.find(\"\\n---\", 3)\n    if end_idx == -1:\n        return\n\n    elapsed = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n    diagnostics_line = (\n        f\"pipeline_diagnostics: \"\n        f\"{{elapsed_seconds: {elapsed:.1f}, \"\n        f\"started_at: \\\"{started_at.isoformat()}\\\", \"\n        f\"finished_at: \\\"{finished_at.isoformat()}\\\"}}\"\n    )\n\n    # Insert before the closing ---\n    new_content = (\n        content[: end_idx]\n        + \"\\n\"\n        + diagnostics_line\n        + content[end_idx:]\n    )\n    output_file.write_text(new_content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n\ndef roadmap_run_step(\n    step: Step,\n    config: PipelineConfig,\n    cancel_check: Callable[[], bool],\n) -> StepResult:\n    \"\"\"Execute a single roadmap step as a Claude subprocess.\n\n    Builds argv with context isolation, launches process, waits with\n    timeout, and returns StepResult.\n    \"\"\"\n    started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n    if config.dry_run:\n        _log.info(\"[dry-run] Would execute step '%s'\", step.id)\n        return StepResult(\n            step=step,\n            status=StepStatus.PASS,\n            attempt=1,\n            started_at=started_at,\n            finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n        )\n\n    # Inline embedding: read input files into the prompt instead of --file flags\n    embedded = _embed_inputs(step.inputs)\n    if embedded and len(embedded.encode(\"utf-8\")) <= _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT:\n        effective_prompt = step.prompt + \"\\n\\n\" + embedded\n        extra_args: list[str] = []\n    elif embedded:\n        _log.warning(\n            \"Step '%s': embedded inputs exceed %d bytes, falling back to --file flags\",\n            step.id,\n            _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT,\n        )\n        effective_prompt = step.prompt\n        extra_args = [\n            arg\n            for input_path in step.inputs\n            for arg in (\"--file\", str(input_path))\n        ]\n    else:\n        effective_prompt = step.prompt\n        extra_args = []\n\n    proc = ClaudeProcess(\n        prompt=effective_prompt,\n        output_file=step.output_file,\n        error_file=step.output_file.with_suffix(\".err\"),\n        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n        model=step.model or config.model,\n        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n        timeout_seconds=step.timeout_seconds,\n        output_format=\"text\",\n        extra_args=extra_args,\n    )\n\n    proc.start()\n\n    # Poll for cancellation while waiting\n    while proc._process is not None and proc._process.poll() is None:\n        if cancel_check():\n            proc.terminate()\n            return StepResult(\n                step=step,\n                status=StepStatus.CANCELLED,\n                attempt=1,\n                gate_failure_reason=\"Cancelled by external signal\",\n                started_at=started_at,\n                finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n            )\n        time.sleep(1)\n\n    exit_code = proc.wait()\n    finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n    if exit_code == 124:\n        return StepResult(\n            step=step,\n            status=StepStatus.TIMEOUT,\n            attempt=1,\n            gate_failure_reason=f\"Step '{step.id}' timed out after {step.timeout_seconds}s\",\n            started_at=started_at,\n            finished_at=finished_at,\n        )\n\n    if exit_code != 0:\n        return StepResult(\n            step=step,\n            status=StepStatus.FAIL,\n            attempt=1,\n            gate_failure_reason=f\"Step '{step.id}' exited with code {exit_code}\",\n            started_at=started_at,\n            finished_at=finished_at,\n        )\n\n    # Sanitize output: strip conversational preamble before gate validation\n    _sanitize_output(step.output_file)\n\n    # Inject executor-populated fields into extract step frontmatter (FR-033)\n    if step.id == \"extract\" and step.output_file.exists():\n        _inject_pipeline_diagnostics(step.output_file, started_at, finished_at)\n\n    # Process completed successfully; gate check happens in execute_pipeline\n    return StepResult(\n        step=step,\n        status=StepStatus.PASS,\n        attempt=1,\n        started_at=started_at,\n        finished_at=finished_at,\n    )\n\n\ndef _build_steps(config: RoadmapConfig) -> list[Step | list[Step]]:\n    \"\"\"Build the 8-step pipeline with parallel generate group.\n\n    Returns a list where each element is either a single Step (sequential)\n    or a list[Step] (parallel group).\n    \"\"\"\n    out = config.output_dir\n\n    # Agent specs\n    agent_a = config.agents[0]\n    agent_b = config.agents[1] if len(config.agents) > 1 else config.agents[0]\n\n    # Output paths\n    extraction = out / \"extraction.md\"\n    roadmap_a = out / f\"roadmap-{agent_a.id}.md\"\n    roadmap_b = out / f\"roadmap-{agent_b.id}.md\"\n    diff_file = out / \"diff-analysis.md\"\n    debate_file = out / \"debate-transcript.md\"\n    score_file = out / \"base-selection.md\"\n    merge_file = out / \"roadmap.md\"\n    test_strat = out / \"test-strategy.md\"\n\n    steps: list[Step | list[Step]] = [\n        # Step 1: Extract\n        Step(\n            id=\"extract\",\n            prompt=build_extract_prompt(config.spec_file),\n            output_file=extraction,\n            gate=EXTRACT_GATE,\n            timeout_seconds=300,\n            inputs=[config.spec_file],\n            retry_limit=1,\n        ),\n        # Steps 2a+2b: Generate (parallel)\n        [\n            Step(\n                id=f\"generate-{agent_a.id}\",\n                prompt=build_generate_prompt(agent_a, extraction),\n                output_file=roadmap_a,\n                gate=GENERATE_A_GATE,\n                timeout_seconds=900,\n                inputs=[extraction],\n                retry_limit=1,\n                model=agent_a.model,\n            ),\n            Step(\n                id=f\"generate-{agent_b.id}\",\n                prompt=build_generate_prompt(agent_b, extraction),\n                output_file=roadmap_b,\n                gate=GENERATE_B_GATE,\n                timeout_seconds=900,\n                inputs=[extraction],\n                retry_limit=1,\n                model=agent_b.model,\n            ),\n        ],\n        # Step 3: Diff\n        Step(\n            id=\"diff\",\n            prompt=build_diff_prompt(roadmap_a, roadmap_b),\n            output_file=diff_file,\n            gate=DIFF_GATE,\n            timeout_seconds=300,\n            inputs=[roadmap_a, roadmap_b],\n            retry_limit=1,\n        ),\n        # Step 4: Debate\n        Step(\n            id=\"debate\",\n            prompt=build_debate_prompt(diff_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b, config.depth),\n            output_file=debate_file,\n            gate=DEBATE_GATE,\n            timeout_seconds=600,\n            inputs=[diff_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b],\n            retry_limit=1,\n        ),\n        # Step 5: Score\n        Step(\n            id=\"score\",\n            prompt=build_score_prompt(debate_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b),\n            output_file=score_file,\n            gate=SCORE_GATE,\n            timeout_seconds=300,\n            inputs=[debate_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b],\n            retry_limit=1,\n        ),\n        # Step 6: Merge\n        Step(\n            id=\"merge\",\n            prompt=build_merge_prompt(score_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b, debate_file),\n            output_file=merge_file,\n            gate=MERGE_GATE,\n            timeout_seconds=600,\n            inputs=[score_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b, debate_file],\n            retry_limit=1,\n        ),\n        # Step 7: Test Strategy\n        Step(\n            id=\"test-strategy\",\n            prompt=build_test_strategy_prompt(merge_file, extraction),\n            output_file=test_strat,\n            gate=TEST_STRATEGY_GATE,\n            timeout_seconds=300,\n            inputs=[merge_file, extraction],\n            retry_limit=1,\n        ),\n    ]\n\n    return steps\n\n\ndef _format_halt_output(results: list[StepResult], config: RoadmapConfig) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Format HALT diagnostic output per spec section 6.2.\"\"\"\n    failed = [r for r in results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)]\n    passed = [r for r in results if r.status == StepStatus.PASS]\n    cancelled = [r for r in results if r.status == StepStatus.CANCELLED]\n\n    if not failed:\n        return \"\"\n\n    fail = failed[-1]\n    step = fail.step\n\n    # Calculate file details if output exists\n    file_info = \"\"\n    if step and step.output_file.exists():\n        content = step.output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        byte_count = len(content.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n        line_count = len(content.splitlines())\n        file_info = f\"  Output size: {byte_count} bytes ({line_count} lines)\\n\"\n    elif step:\n        file_info = \"  Output file: not created\\n\"\n\n    elapsed = f\"{fail.duration_seconds:.0f}s\"\n\n    lines = [\n        f\"ERROR: Roadmap pipeline halted at step '{step.id}' (attempt {fail.attempt}/2)\",\n        f\"  Gate failure: {fail.gate_failure_reason}\",\n        f\"  Output file: {step.output_file}\",\n        file_info.rstrip(),\n        f\"  Step timeout: {step.timeout_seconds}s | Elapsed: {elapsed}\",\n        \"\",\n        f\"Completed steps: {', '.join(f'{r.step.id} (PASS, attempt {r.attempt})' for r in passed) or 'none'}\",\n        f\"Failed step:     {step.id} ({fail.status.value}, attempt {fail.attempt})\",\n    ]\n\n    # Collect skipped steps\n    all_step_ids = _get_all_step_ids(config)\n    executed_ids = {r.step.id for r in results}\n    skipped_ids = [sid for sid in all_step_ids if sid not in executed_ids]\n    if cancelled:\n        skipped_ids = [r.step.id for r in cancelled] + skipped_ids\n\n    if skipped_ids:\n        lines.append(f\"Skipped steps:   {', '.join(skipped_ids)}\")\n\n    lines.extend([\n        \"\",\n        \"To retry from this step:\",\n        f\"  superclaude roadmap run {config.spec_file} --resume\",\n        \"\",\n        \"To inspect the failing output:\",\n        f\"  cat {step.output_file}\",\n    ])\n\n    return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n\ndef _get_all_step_ids(config: RoadmapConfig) -> list[str]:\n    \"\"\"Get all step IDs in pipeline order.\"\"\"\n    agent_a = config.agents[0]\n    agent_b = config.agents[1] if len(config.agents) > 1 else config.agents[0]\n    return [\n        \"extract\",\n        f\"generate-{agent_a.id}\",\n        f\"generate-{agent_b.id}\",\n        \"diff\",\n        \"debate\",\n        \"score\",\n        \"merge\",\n        \"test-strategy\",\n    ]\n\n\ndef _print_step_start(step: Step) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Print progress output when a step starts.\"\"\"\n    print(f\"[roadmap] Starting step: {step.id}\", flush=True)\n\n\ndef _print_step_complete(step: Step, result: StepResult) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Print progress output when a step completes.\"\"\"\n    duration = f\"{result.duration_seconds:.0f}s\"\n    if result.status == StepStatus.PASS:\n        print(\n            f\"[roadmap] Step {step.id}  PASS (attempt {result.attempt}, {duration})\",\n            flush=True,\n        )\n    else:\n        print(\n            f\"[roadmap] Step {step.id}  {result.status.value} (attempt {result.attempt}, {duration})\",\n            flush=True,\n        )\n        if result.gate_failure_reason:\n            print(f\"           Reason: {result.gate_failure_reason}\", flush=True)\n\n\ndef _dry_run_output(steps: list[Step | list[Step]]) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Print step plan and gate criteria for --dry-run.\"\"\"\n    step_num = 0\n    for entry in steps:\n        if isinstance(entry, list):\n            for s in entry:\n                step_num += 1\n                _print_step_plan(step_num, s, parallel=True)\n        else:\n            step_num += 1\n            _print_step_plan(step_num, entry)\n\n\ndef _print_step_plan(num: int, step: Step, parallel: bool = False) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Print a single step's plan entry.\"\"\"\n    par_label = \" (parallel)\" if parallel else \"\"\n    print(f\"Step {num}{par_label}: {step.id}\")\n    print(f\"  Output: {step.output_file}\")\n    print(f\"  Timeout: {step.timeout_seconds}s\")\n    if step.model:\n        print(f\"  Model: {step.model}\")\n    if step.gate:\n        print(f\"  Gate tier: {step.gate.enforcement_tier}\")\n        print(f\"  Gate min_lines: {step.gate.min_lines}\")\n        if step.gate.required_frontmatter_fields:\n            print(f\"  Gate frontmatter: {', '.join(step.gate.required_frontmatter_fields)}\")\n        if step.gate.semantic_checks:\n            checks = [c.name for c in step.gate.semantic_checks]\n            print(f\"  Semantic checks: {', '.join(checks)}\")\n    print()\n\n\ndef _save_state(config: RoadmapConfig, results: list[StepResult]) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Write .roadmap-state.json to output_dir via atomic tmp + os.replace().\n\n    Preserves existing ``validation`` key if present (written by\n    ``_save_validation_status``).\n    \"\"\"\n    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n    spec_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n\n    # Preserve existing validation status across state rewrites\n    existing = read_state(state_file)\n    existing_validation = existing.get(\"validation\") if existing else None\n\n    state = {\n        \"schema_version\": 1,\n        \"spec_file\": str(config.spec_file),\n        \"spec_hash\": spec_hash,\n        \"agents\": [{\"model\": a.model, \"persona\": a.persona} for a in config.agents],\n        \"depth\": config.depth,\n        \"last_run\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n        \"steps\": {\n            r.step.id: {\n                \"status\": r.status.value,\n                \"attempt\": r.attempt,\n                \"output_file\": str(r.step.output_file),\n                \"started_at\": r.started_at.isoformat(),\n                \"completed_at\": r.finished_at.isoformat(),\n            }\n            for r in results\n            if r.step\n        },\n    }\n\n    if existing_validation is not None:\n        state[\"validation\"] = existing_validation\n\n    write_state(state, state_file)\n\n\ndef write_state(state: dict, path: Path) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Write state dict to path atomically via tmp file + os.replace().\"\"\"\n    import os as _os\n\n    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    tmp = path.with_suffix(\".tmp\")\n    tmp.write_text(json.dumps(state, indent=2), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    _os.replace(str(tmp), str(path))\n\n\ndef read_state(path: Path) -> dict | None:\n    \"\"\"Read state from path with graceful recovery on missing/malformed files.\"\"\"\n    if not path.exists():\n        return None\n    try:\n        text = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        if not text.strip():\n            return None\n        return json.loads(text)\n    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n        return None\n\n\ndef apply_decomposition_pass(deliverables: list[Deliverable]) -> list[Deliverable]:\n    \"\"\"Post-generation decomposition pass for the roadmap pipeline.\n\n    Runs after deliverable generation, before output formatting.\n    Splits behavioral deliverables into Implement/Verify pairs.\n\n    Idempotent: running twice produces identical results because\n    already-decomposed deliverables (IDs ending .a/.b) are skipped.\n\n    Preserves milestone-internal ordering: deliverables within each\n    milestone maintain their relative order after decomposition.\n    \"\"\"\n    return decompose_deliverables(deliverables)\n\n\ndef execute_roadmap(config: RoadmapConfig, resume: bool = False, no_validate: bool = False) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Execute the roadmap generation pipeline.\n\n    Builds the step list, handles --dry-run and --resume, then\n    delegates to execute_pipeline() with roadmap_run_step.\n\n    After all 8 steps pass, auto-invokes validation unless\n    --no-validate is set or --resume halted on a failed step.\n    \"\"\"\n    config.output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    steps = _build_steps(config)\n\n    # --dry-run: print plan and exit\n    if config.dry_run:\n        _dry_run_output(steps)\n        return\n\n    # --resume: check which steps already pass their gates\n    if resume:\n        from ..pipeline.gates import gate_passed\n\n        steps = _apply_resume(steps, config, gate_passed)\n\n    # Execute pipeline\n    results = execute_pipeline(\n        steps=steps,\n        config=config,\n        run_step=roadmap_run_step,\n        on_step_start=_print_step_start,\n        on_step_complete=_print_step_complete,\n    )\n\n    # Save state\n    _save_state(config, results)\n\n    # Check for failures\n    failures = [r for r in results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)]\n    if failures:\n        halt_msg = _format_halt_output(results, config)\n        print(halt_msg, file=sys.stderr)\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    print(f\"\\n[roadmap] Pipeline complete: {len(results)} steps passed\", flush=True)\n\n    # Auto-invoke validation after successful pipeline completion\n    if no_validate:\n        print(\"[roadmap] Validation skipped (--no-validate)\", flush=True)\n        _save_validation_status(config, \"skipped\")\n        return\n\n    # Check if validation already completed (--resume path)\n    if resume:\n        state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n        state = read_state(state_file)\n        if state and \"validation\" in state:\n            saved = state[\"validation\"]\n            if saved.get(\"status\") in (\"pass\", \"fail\"):\n                print(\n                    f\"[roadmap] Validation already completed ({saved['status']}), skipping\",\n                    flush=True,\n                )\n                return\n\n    _auto_invoke_validate(config)\n\n\ndef _save_validation_status(\n    config: RoadmapConfig,\n    status: str,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Update .roadmap-state.json with validation status.\n\n    Adds or updates the ``validation`` key without modifying existing state.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    config:\n        RoadmapConfig with output_dir.\n    status:\n        One of \"pass\", \"fail\", or \"skipped\".\n    \"\"\"\n    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n    state = read_state(state_file)\n    if state is None:\n        state = {}\n    state[\"validation\"] = {\n        \"status\": status,\n        \"timestamp\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n    }\n    write_state(state, state_file)\n\n\ndef _auto_invoke_validate(config: RoadmapConfig) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Auto-invoke validation after a successful roadmap pipeline run.\n\n    Inherits --model, --max-turns, --debug from the parent roadmap config.\n    Default agent count for roadmap-run invocation is 2 (dual-agent for rigor).\n    \"\"\"\n    from .models import AgentSpec, ValidateConfig\n    from .validate_executor import execute_validate\n\n    # Default to 2 agents for roadmap-run auto-invocation (dual-agent for rigor per OQ-1)\n    validate_agents = config.agents[:2] if len(config.agents) >= 2 else config.agents\n\n    validate_config = ValidateConfig(\n        output_dir=config.output_dir,\n        agents=validate_agents,\n        work_dir=config.output_dir,\n        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n        model=config.model,\n        debug=config.debug,\n    )\n\n    print(\"\\n[roadmap] Auto-invoking validation...\", flush=True)\n\n    try:\n        counts = execute_validate(validate_config)\n        blocking = counts.get(\"blocking_count\", 0)\n        warning = counts.get(\"warning_count\", 0)\n        info = counts.get(\"info_count\", 0)\n        print(\n            f\"[validate] Complete: {blocking} blocking, {warning} warning, {info} info\",\n            flush=True,\n        )\n        validation_status = \"fail\" if blocking > 0 else \"pass\"\n        _save_validation_status(config, validation_status)\n    except FileNotFoundError as exc:\n        _log.error(\"Validation skipped: %s\", exc)\n        print(f\"[validate] Skipped: {exc}\", file=sys.stderr, flush=True)\n        _save_validation_status(config, \"skipped\")\n\n\ndef _apply_resume(\n    steps: list[Step | list[Step]],\n    config: RoadmapConfig,\n    gate_fn: Callable,\n) -> list[Step | list[Step]]:\n    \"\"\"Apply --resume logic: skip steps whose outputs already pass gates.\n\n    Also checks for stale spec detection.\n    \"\"\"\n    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n    state = read_state(state_file)\n    force_extract = False\n    if state is not None:\n        saved_hash = state.get(\"spec_hash\", \"\")\n        current_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n        if saved_hash and saved_hash != current_hash:\n            print(\n                f\"WARNING: spec-file has changed since last run.\\n\"\n                f\"  Last hash: {saved_hash[:12]}...\\n\"\n                f\"  Current:   {current_hash[:12]}...\\n\"\n                f\"Forcing re-run of extract step.\",\n                file=sys.stderr,\n                flush=True,\n            )\n            force_extract = True\n\n    skipped = 0\n    result: list[Step | list[Step]] = []\n    found_failure = False\n\n    for entry in steps:\n        if found_failure:\n            # After first failing step, include all remaining steps\n            result.append(entry)\n            continue\n\n        if isinstance(entry, list):\n            # Parallel group: check all steps\n            all_pass = True\n            for s in entry:\n                if s.gate:\n                    passed, _reason = gate_fn(s.output_file, s.gate)\n                    if not passed:\n                        all_pass = False\n                        break\n                else:\n                    all_pass = False\n                    break\n            if all_pass:\n                skipped += len(entry)\n                print(f\"[roadmap] Skipping {', '.join(s.id for s in entry)} (gates pass)\", flush=True)\n            else:\n                found_failure = True\n                result.append(entry)\n        else:\n            # Force re-run of extract on stale spec\n            if force_extract and entry.id == \"extract\":\n                found_failure = True\n                result.append(entry)\n                continue\n\n            if entry.gate:\n                passed, _reason = gate_fn(entry.output_file, entry.gate)\n                if passed:\n                    skipped += 1\n                    print(f\"[roadmap] Skipping {entry.id} (gate passes)\", flush=True)\n                    continue\n            found_failure = True\n            result.append(entry)\n\n    if skipped > 0:\n        print(f\"[roadmap] Skipped {skipped} steps (gates pass)\", flush=True)\n\n    if not result:\n        print(\"[roadmap] All steps already pass gates. Nothing to do.\", flush=True)\n\n    return result\n```\n\n## Key function signatures and patterns\n\n### Core signatures\n- `roadmap_run_step(step: Step, config: PipelineConfig, cancel_check: Callable[[], bool]) -> StepResult`\n- `_build_steps(config: RoadmapConfig) -> list[Step | list[Step]]`\n- `execute_roadmap(config: RoadmapConfig, resume: bool = False, no_validate: bool = False) -> None`\n- `_apply_resume(steps: list[Step | list[Step]], config: RoadmapConfig, gate_fn: Callable) -> list[Step | list[Step]]`\n\n### Support signatures\n- `_embed_inputs(input_paths: list[Path]) -> str`\n- `_sanitize_output(output_file: Path) -> int`\n- `_inject_pipeline_diagnostics(output_file: Path, started_at: datetime, finished_at: datetime) -> None`\n- `_format_halt_output(results: list[StepResult], config: RoadmapConfig) -> str`\n- `_get_all_step_ids(config: RoadmapConfig) -> list[str]`\n- `_print_step_start(step: Step) -> None`\n- `_print_step_complete(step: Step, result: StepResult) -> None`\n- `_dry_run_output(steps: list[Step | list[Step]]) -> None`\n- `_print_step_plan(num: int, step: Step, parallel: bool = False) -> None`\n- `_save_state(config: RoadmapConfig, results: list[StepResult]) -> None`\n- `write_state(state: dict, path: Path) -> None`\n- `read_state(path: Path) -> dict | None`\n- `apply_decomposition_pass(deliverables: list[Deliverable]) -> list[Deliverable]`\n- `_save_validation_status(config: RoadmapConfig, status: str) -> None`\n- `_auto_invoke_validate(config: RoadmapConfig) -> None`\n\n## How the pipeline steps are built\n\n### Central builder\n`_build_steps(config)` is the authoritative step assembly function.\n\n### Step assembly pattern\nIt constructs:\n- output paths first\n- prompt for each step via prompt builder\n- gate for each step via gate constants\n- timeout/input/model/retry metadata inline\n\n### Sequence shape\nThe return type is:\n- `list[Step | list[Step]]`\n\nThis encodes:\n- sequential step → `Step`\n- parallel group → `list[Step]`\n\n### Current pipeline layout in practice\nDespite the module docstring saying “8-step roadmap pipeline,” `_build_steps()` currently returns 7 top-level entries:\n1. `extract`\n2. `[generate-A, generate-B]` as one parallel group\n3. `diff`\n4. `debate`\n5. `score`\n6. `merge`\n7. `test-strategy`\n\nBut if you count individual step IDs, it is 8 steps:\n1. extract\n2. generate-A\n3. generate-B\n4. diff\n5. debate\n6. score\n7. merge\n8. test-strategy\n\n### Concrete construction pattern\nExample:\n\n```python\nStep(\n    id=\"diff\",\n    prompt=build_diff_prompt(roadmap_a, roadmap_b),\n    output_file=diff_file,\n    gate=DIFF_GATE,\n    timeout_seconds=300,\n    inputs=[roadmap_a, roadmap_b],\n    retry_limit=1,\n)\n```\n\n### Parallel generate pattern\nThe generate phase is the only parallel group:\n\n```python\n[\n    Step(id=f\"generate-{agent_a.id}\", ... gate=GENERATE_A_GATE ... model=agent_a.model),\n    Step(id=f\"generate-{agent_b.id}\", ... gate=GENERATE_B_GATE ... model=agent_b.model),\n]\n```\n\n### Agent fallback behavior\nIf only one agent is configured:\n```python\nagent_b = config.agents[1] if len(config.agents) > 1 else config.agents[0]\n```\nSo the second generate step still exists, but may duplicate agent A.\n\n## Execution behavior patterns relevant to Phase 3\n\n### Prompt/input isolation\nThe prompt builders do not read files. Instead executor does:\n- inline embed file content if total embedded size <= 100KB\n- otherwise fall back to `--file <path>`\n\n### Output sanitation\nAfter subprocess success:\n- `_sanitize_output(step.output_file)` strips any prose before first `---`\n- extract step additionally gets `pipeline_diagnostics` injected into frontmatter\n\n### Gate timing\nThe comment explicitly says:\n- subprocess success only returns `StepStatus.PASS`\n- actual gate enforcement happens later in `execute_pipeline`\n\n### Resume model\nResume skips steps whose existing output files already pass their gates.\nFor parallel groups, all members must pass or the whole group reruns.\n\n---\n\n# 5) `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/roadmap/fidelity.py`\n\n## Full file contents\n\n```python\n\"\"\"Fidelity deviation data model -- 7-column deviation report schema.\n\nImplements the canonical deviation report schema defined in D-0003 (OQ-006)\nand docs/reference/deviation-report-format.md as a Python dataclass.\n\nThis module has zero imports from pipeline or gate enforcement code.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom enum import Enum\n\n\nclass Severity(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Deviation severity classification per D-0016.\"\"\"\n\n    HIGH = \"HIGH\"\n    MEDIUM = \"MEDIUM\"\n    LOW = \"LOW\"\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass FidelityDeviation:\n    \"\"\"A single deviation row from a fidelity report.\n\n    Represents one row of the 7-column deviation table defined in the\n    canonical schema (D-0003, docs/reference/deviation-report-format.md).\n\n    Fields:\n        id: Unique deviation identifier, format DEV-NNN (zero-padded 3-digit).\n        severity: Classification level (HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW).\n        deviation: Concise description of what differs.\n        upstream_quote: Verbatim quote from the upstream document.\n        downstream_quote: Verbatim quote from the downstream document,\n            or '[MISSING]' if absent.\n        impact: Assessment of how the deviation affects correctness.\n        recommended_correction: Specific action to resolve the deviation.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    id: str\n    severity: Severity\n    deviation: str\n    upstream_quote: str\n    downstream_quote: str\n    impact: str\n    recommended_correction: str\n\n    def __post_init__(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Validate field constraints.\"\"\"\n        if not isinstance(self.severity, Severity):\n            raise TypeError(\n                f\"severity must be a Severity enum member, got {type(self.severity).__name__}\"\n            )\n        if not self.id:\n            raise ValueError(\"id must be non-empty\")\n        if not self.deviation:\n            raise ValueError(\"deviation must be non-empty\")\n        if not self.upstream_quote:\n            raise ValueError(\"upstream_quote must be non-empty\")\n        if not self.downstream_quote:\n            raise ValueError(\"downstream_quote must be non-empty\")\n        if not self.impact:\n            raise ValueError(\"impact must be non-empty\")\n        if not self.recommended_correction:\n            raise ValueError(\"recommended_correction must be non-empty\")\n```\n\n## Key function/class signatures and patterns\n\n### Enum\n- `class Severity(Enum):`\n  - `HIGH = \"HIGH\"`\n  - `MEDIUM = \"MEDIUM\"`\n  - `LOW = \"LOW\"`\n\n### Dataclass\n- `@dataclass`\n- `class FidelityDeviation:`\n  - fields:\n    - `id: str`\n    - `severity: Severity`\n    - `deviation: str`\n    - `upstream_quote: str`\n    - `downstream_quote: str`\n    - `impact: str`\n    - `recommended_correction: str`\n- validation:\n  - `def __post_init__(self) -> None`\n\n## Pattern relevance\nThis is currently only a data model, not parser/enforcer logic.\nIt suggests Phase 3 may need:\n- fidelity report parsing\n- mapping report rows into `FidelityDeviation`\n- severity-based counting\n- then gate checks based on frontmatter or parsed rows\n\n---\n\n# 6) `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/tests/roadmap/test_gates_data.py`\n\n## Full file contents\n\n```python\n\"\"\"Tests for roadmap/gates.py -- gate criteria data definitions and semantic checks.\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport pytest\n\nfrom superclaude.cli.roadmap.gates import (\n    ALL_GATES,\n    DEBATE_GATE,\n    DIFF_GATE,\n    EXTRACT_GATE,\n    GENERATE_A_GATE,\n    GENERATE_B_GATE,\n    MERGE_GATE,\n    SCORE_GATE,\n    TEST_STRATEGY_GATE,\n    _convergence_score_valid,\n    _cross_refs_resolve,\n    _frontmatter_values_non_empty,\n    _has_actionable_content,\n    _high_severity_count_zero,\n    _no_duplicate_headings,\n    _no_heading_gaps,\n    _tasklist_ready_consistent,\n)\nfrom superclaude.cli.pipeline.models import GateCriteria\n\n\nclass TestGateInstances:\n    def test_all_gates_are_gate_criteria(self):\n        for name, gate in `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/tests/roadmap/test_fidelity.py`\n\n## Full file contents\n\n```python\n\"\"\"Tests for roadmap/fidelity.py -- FidelityDeviation dataclass.\"\"\"\n\nfrom Severity.HIGH.value == \"HIGH\"\n\n    def test_medium(self):\n        assert Severity.MEDIUM.value == \"MEDIUM\"\n\n    def test_low(self):\n        assert Severity.LOW.value == \"LOW\"\n\n    def test_three_members(self):\n        assert len(Severity) == 3\n\n\nclass TestFidelityDeviationDataclass:\n    def _make_deviation(self, **overrides):\n        defaults = {\n            \"id\": \"DEV-001\",\n            \"severity\": Severity.HIGH,\n            \"deviation\": \"Missing FR-019\",\n            \"upstream_quote\": \"Cross-references must be validated\",\n            \"downstream_quote\": \"[MISSING]\",\n            \"impact\": \"No cross-ref integrity\",\n            \"recommended_correction\": \"Add FR-019 to Phase 2\",\n        }\n        defaults.update(overrides)\n        return FidelityDeviation(**defaults)\n\n    def test_fidelity_deviation_dataclass(self):\n        \"\"\"Basic construction with valid fields.\"\"\"\n        dev = self._make_deviation()\n        assert dev.id == \"DEV-001\"\n        assert dev.severity == Severity.HIGH\n        assert dev.deviation == \"Missing FR-019\"\n        assert dev.upstream_quote == \"Cross-references must be validated\"\n        assert dev.downstream_quote == \"[MISSING]\"\n        assert dev.impact == \"No cross-ref integrity\"\n        assert dev.recommended_correction == \"Add FR-019 to Phase 2\"\n\n    def test_fidelity_deviation_all_seven_fields(self):\n        \"\"\"Dataclass has exactly 7 fields matching canonical schema.\"\"\"\n        import dataclasses\n \"severity\",\n            \"deviation\",\n            \"upstream_quote\",\n            \"downstream_quote\",\n            \"impact\",\n            \"recommended_correction\",\n        ]\n\n    def      )\n\n    def test_fidelity_deviation_medium_severity(self):\n        \"\"\"MEDIUM severity constructs correctly.\"\"\"\n        dev = self._make_deviation(severity=Severity.MEDIUM)\n        assert dev.severity == Severity.MEDIUM\n\n    def test_fidelity_deviation_low_severity(self):\n        \"\"\"LOW severity constructs correctly.\"\"\"\n        dev = self._make_deviation(severity=Severity.LOW)\n        assert dev.severity == Severity.LOW\n\n    def test_fidelity_deviation_empty_id_raises(self):\n        \"\"\"Empty id raises ValueError.\"\"\"\n       assert dev.downstream_quote == \"[MISSING]\"\n\n    def test_fidelity_deviation_importable(self):\n        \"\"\"Dataclass is importable from the fidelity module.\"\"\"\n        from superclaude.cli.roadmap.fidelity import FidelityDeviation as FD\n        assert FD is FidelityDeviation\n```\n\n## Test patterns\n\n### Coverage focus\n- enum values\n- exact dataclass field order/count\n- `__post_init__` validation behavior\n- permissibility of `\"[MISSING]\"` downstream quote\n\n### Implication\nCurrent fidelity support is intentionally minimal and schema-centric:\n- data model exists\n- tests validate data shape\n- no parser/generator/report gate yet in this file set\n\n---\n\n# Cross-file synthesis\n\n## A) How prompts are structured\n\nThe prompt system in `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/roadmap/prompts.py` follows a strong template:\n\n1. Role declaration\n   - `\"You are a ... specialist\"`\n\n2. Input instruction\n   - `\"Read the provided ...\"`\n\n3. Mandatory YAML frontmatter block\n   - explicit list of required fields\n   - exact type/meaning hints\n\n4. Structured body specification\n   - enumerated list or heading list\n   - strong shape constraints\n\n5. Shared format enforcement tail\n   - `_OUTPUT_FORMAT_BLOCK`\n\nThis means if you add a Phase 3 prompt, the established pattern is:\n- pure function\n- no I/O\n- explicit frontmatter spec\n- explicit section structure\n- append `_OUTPUT_FORMAT_BLOCK`\n\n## B) How gates are defined and registered\n\nGate definition pattern:\n- module-level `GateCriteria(...)` constants\n- semantic checks attached as `SemanticCheck(...)`\n- no mutable/global registry object\n- ordered registry is a list of tuples (`ALL_GATES`) when needed\n\nKey registration styles:\n- roadmap pipeline gates live in `gates.py`\n- validation pipeline gates live in `validate_gates.py`\n- validate gates can import reusable semantic helpers from roadmap gates\n\n## C) How pipeline steps are built\n\n`/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/roadmap/executor.py` uses `_build_steps(config)`.\n\nPattern:\n- compute output file paths once\n- map step → prompt builder\n- map step → gate constant\n- include:\n  - `id`\n  - `prompt`\n  - `output_file`\n  - `gate`\n  - `timeout_seconds`\n  - `inputs`\n  - `retry_limit`\n  - optional `model`\n\nParallelism is represented structurally by nesting:\n- `Step`\n- `list[Step]`\n\nThat is the main pipeline composition idiom.\n\n## D) Important current fidelity-related state\n\nThere is an early fidelity foundation but not a full subsystem yet:\n\n### Present\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/roadmap/fidelity.py`\n  - Prompt builders are pure string factories with strict YAML-first contracts.\n2. gate criteria -- data definitions for each pipeline step's gate.\n\nThis module defines GateCriteria instances as module-level constants.\nGate criteria are pure data -- no logic, no imports from pipeline/gates.py\nenforcement code (NFR-005).\n\nSemantic check functions are defined here as pure functions accepting\nfile content and returning bool. They are registered on the STRICT-tier\nGateCriteria instances.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom ..pipeline.models import GateCriteria, SemanticCheck\n\n\n# --- Semantic check functions (pure: content -> bool) ---\n\ndef _no_heading_gaps(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Verify heading levels increment by at most 1 (no H2 -> H4 skip).\"\"\"\n    prev_level = 0\n    for line in content.splitlines():\n        stripped = line.lstrip()\n        if stripped.startswith(\"#\"):\n            level = 0\n            for ch in stripped:\n                if ch == \"#\":\n                    level += 1\n                else:\n                    break\n            if prev_level > 0 and level > prev_level + 1:\n                return False\n            prev_level = level\n    return True\n\n\ndef _cross_refs_resolve(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Verify all 'See section' / cross-reference patterns have matching headings.\n\n    Checks that internal references like 'See section X.Y' or 'See X.Y'\n    have corresponding headings. Unresolved references emit warnings but\n    do not block the pipeline (warning-only mode per OQ-001 for v2.20).\n    \"\"\"\n    import re\n    import warnings\n\n    # Extract heading anchors (simplified: heading text, lowercased)\n    headings: set[str] = set()\n    for line in content.splitlines():\n        stripped = line.lstrip()\n        if stripped.startswith(\"#\"):\n            heading_text = stripped.lstrip(\"#\").strip().lower()\n            headings.add(heading_text)\n\n    # Find cross-references like \"See section X\" or \"(see X.Y)\"\n    refs = re.findall(r'[Ss]ee\\s+(?:[Ss]ection\\s+)?[\"\\']?(\\d+(?:\\.\\d+)*)', content)\n    # If there are no cross-references, that's fine\n    if not refs:\n        return True\n\n    # Check each reference against headings\n    unresolved: list[str] = []\n    for ref in refs:\n        found = any(ref in h for h in headings)\n        if not found:\n            # Also check if the section number appears as a heading prefix\n            found = any(h.startswith(ref) for h in headings)\n        if not found:\n            unresolved.append(ref)\n\n    if unresolved:\n        for ref in unresolved:\n            warnings.warn(\n                f\"Unresolved cross-reference: 'See section {ref}' has no matching heading\",\n                stacklevel=2,\n            )\n        # Warning-only mode (OQ-001): return True to avoid blocking pipeline\n        return True\n\n    return True\n\n\ndef _no_duplicate_headings(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"No duplicate H2 or H3 heading text.\"\"\"\n    seen: dict[int, set[str]] = {2: set(), 3: set()}\n    for line in content.splitlines():\n        stripped = line.lstrip()\n        if stripped.startswith(\"## \") and not stripped.startswith(\"### \"):\n            text = stripped[3:].strip().lower()\n            if text in seen[2]:\n                return False\n            seen[2].add(text)\n        elif stripped.startswith(\"### \"):\n            text = stripped[4:].strip().lower()\n            if text in seen[3]:\n                return False\n            seen[3].add(text)\n    return True\n\n\ndef _frontmatter_values_non_empty(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"All YAML frontmatter fields have non-empty values.\"\"\"\n    stripped = content.lstrip()\n    if not stripped.startswith(\"---\"):\n        return False\n\n    rest = stripped[3:].lstrip(\"\\n\")\n    end_idx = rest.find(\"\\n---\")\n    if end_idx == -1:\n        return False\n\n    frontmatter_text = rest[:end_idx]\n    for line in frontmatter_text.splitlines():\n        line = line.strip()\n        if \":\" in line:\n            _key, value = line.split(\":\", 1)\n            if not value.strip():\n                return False\n    return True\n\n\ndef _has_actionable_content(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"At least one section contains numbered or bulleted items.\"\"\"\n    import re\n    # Returns False if:\n    - Frontmatter is missing\n    - high_severity_count field is missing\n    - high_severity_count > 0\n\n    Raises TypeError if high_severity_count value is not parseable as an integer.\n    \"\"\"\n    fm = _parse_frontmatter(content)\n    if fm is None:\n        return False\n\n    value = fm.get(\"high_severity_count\")\n    if value is None:\n        return False\n\n    try:\n        count = int(value)\n    except (ValueError, TypeError):\n        raise TypeError(\n            f\"high_severity_count must be an integer, got: {value!r}\"\n        )\n\n    return count == 0\n\n\ndef _tasklist_ready_consistent(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Validate tasklist_ready is consistent with severity counts.\n\n    Consistency rule: tasklist_ready=true requires high_severity_count=0\n    AND validation_complete=true. If tasklist_ready=true but either\n    condition fails, this returns False (inconsistency detected).\n\n    Returns False if:\n    - Frontmatter is missing\n    - tasklist_ready field is missing\n    - Inconsistency: tasklist_ready=true but high_severity_count > 0\n    - Inconsistency: tasklist_ready=true but validation_complete != true\n    \"\"\"\n    fm = _parse_frontmatter(content)\n    if fm is None:\n        return False\n\n    tasklist_ready_str = fm.get(\"tasklist_ready\")\n    if tasklist_ready_str is None:\n        return False\n\n    tasklist_ready = tasklist_ready_str.lower() == \"true\"\n\n    if not tasklist_ready:\n        # If tasklist_ready is false, that's always consistent\n        return True\n\n    # tasklist_ready is true -- verify consistency\n    high_str = fm.get(\"high_severity_count\")\n    if high_str is None:\n        return False\n    try:\n        high_count = int(high_str)\n    except (ValueError, TypeError):\n        return False\n\n    if high_count > 0:\n        return False\n\n    validation_str = fm.get(\"validation_complete\")\n    if validation_str is None:\n        return False\n    if validation_str.lower() != \"true\":\n content.lstrip()\n    if not stripped.startswith(\"---\"):\n        return False\n\n    rest = stripped[3:].lstrip(\"\\n\")\n    end_idx = rest.find(\"\\n---\")\n    if end_idx == -1:\n        return formatting.\n   562→    Splits behavioral deliverables into Implement/Verify pairs.\n   563→\n   564→    Idempotent: running twice produces identical results because\n   565→    already-decomposed deliverables (IDs ending .a/.b) are skipped.\n   566→\n   567→    Preserves milestone-internal ordering: deliverables within each\n   568→    milestone maintain their relative order after
